pages.title: Welcome! | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Welcome page introducing Blockchain Web Services (BWS), a platform that makes blockchain solutions accessible to non-technical users. Covers Platform APIs (IPFS Upload, Blockchain Save/Hash, NFT.zK) and Marketplace Solutions (Blockchain Badges, FAN Game Cube, ESG Credits, X Bot). Introduces BWS Developer Grants program with bounty payments and API fee sharing.
useCases: Store data on blockchain with Certificate of Trust
Upload NFT images and JSON files to IPFS network
Create and transfer NFTs to non-blockchain users
Issue secure blockchain-based digital credentials
Engage sports fans with NFT-based digital assets tied to field locations
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Developer Grants Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Welcome! Blockchain Web Services documentation. Blockchain Web Services (aka BWS) aims to transform the blockchain market by making innovative solutions accessible to a broad audience, including those without technical or Web3 experience. Read Blockchain Web Services's White Paper arrow-up-right . circle-info NFT Game Cube's new marketplace solution launch! BWS’s NFT Game Cube lets sports fans own digital assets tied to specific sections of a sports field—not only football but chess, tennis, cricket, and many more. As real-time events unfold at their location during the game, fans earn points, unlocking exciting rewards while creating a new revenue stream for sports clubs, former athletes, influencers, and anyone with a market in the sports industry. Read the Launch Pitch arrow-up-right . hashtag Platform API By leveraging the Blockchain Web Services API, developers can seamlessly integrate blockchain technology into their offerings, providing their target customers with blockchain innovative solutions without requiring Web3 expertise. Blockchain Save Easily and affordably store data on the blockchain while receiving a Certificate of Trust for enhanced transparency. Blockchain Hash A single API call to save data to the blockchain without passing your wallet or managing the transaction fees. IPFS Effortlessly upload images and JSON files to the IPFS network and use our IPFS gateway for reliable access. NFT.zk This solution is designed to create NFTs easily but also to transfer the ownership to non-blockchain-experts users that will now be able to experience the NFT revolution too. hashtag Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Web Services is not just an API but a set of platform protocols and development practices to enable the community to contribute and grow the solutions offering. The following solutions offer mass market blockchain use cases targeting end-customers without Web3 know-how or setup. Blockchain Badges Blockchain Badges elevate your credentialing system with secure, immutable digital recognition on the blockchain. FAN Game Cube Engage your fans and get a new revenue stream for your Sports Club by combining NFTs and Machine Learning. ESG Credits Assists financial institutions and system integrators in delivering environmental impact reporting to their digital channels. X Bot A Telegram bot that helps community groups boost engagement on X (formerly Twitter) and track project's KOLs. hashtag BWS Developer Grants Recognizing the importance of building a vibrant developer ecosystem, we are excited to introduce the BWS Developer Grants—a program designed to incentivize developers to create tools and applications on top of our platform protocols, rewarding them for their contributions. The initiative will maintain a development fund to support growth efforts, focusing on fostering innovation within the BWS ecosystem. Bounty Payments Developers whose projects are selected for integration with BWS will receive bounty payments based on their complexity and impact. Recognition and Exposure Featured projects will be showcased on the BWS website, social media, and promotional channels, giving developers visibility and recognition within the blockchain community. Technical Support and Resources The BWS team will provide developers access to technical support, detailed documentation, and resources to streamline development. API Fees Once your application is published, you will receive a fee in $BWS tokens every time your APIs get called. Read the BWS White Paper arrow-up-right to learn more about developer grants and $BWS tokenomics. Next Quick Start chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago Platform API Marketplace Solutions BWS Developer Grants sun-bright desktop moon sun-bright desktop moon

paragraphs: Blockchain Web Services documentation.

By leveraging the Blockchain Web Services API, developers can seamlessly integrate blockchain technology into their offerings, providing their target customers with blockchain innovative solutions without requiring Web3 expertise.
Easily and affordably store data on the blockchain while receiving a Certificate of Trust for enhanced transparency.
A single API call to save data to the blockchain without passing your wallet or managing the transaction fees.
Effortlessly upload images and JSON files to the IPFS network and use our IPFS gateway for reliable access.
This solution is designed to create NFTs easily but also to transfer the ownership to non-blockchain-experts users that will now be able to experience the NFT revolution too.

Blockchain Web Services is not just an API but a set of platform protocols and development practices to enable the community to contribute and grow the solutions offering.

The following solutions offer mass market blockchain use cases targeting end-customers without Web3 know-how or setup.
Blockchain Badges elevate your credentialing system with secure, immutable digital recognition on the blockchain.
Engage your fans and get a new revenue stream for your Sports Club by combining NFTs and Machine Learning.
Assists financial institutions and system integrators in delivering environmental impact reporting to their digital channels.
A Telegram bot that helps community groups boost engagement on X (formerly Twitter) and track project's KOLs.

Recognizing the importance of building a vibrant developer ecosystem, we are excited to introduce the BWS Developer Grants—a program designed to incentivize developers to create tools and applications on top of our platform protocols, rewarding them for their contributions.
The initiative will maintain a development fund to support growth efforts, focusing on fostering innovation within the BWS ecosystem.

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right API Endpoint Authentication chevron-right Main API Methods chevron-right API Responses chevron-right Error Status Codes Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down API How Tos API Responses statusCode, statusMessage, info When calling BWS API, you can get an HTTP arrow-up-right layer transport error arrow-up-right , that is, an error that has occurred before our code is executed (for example, 503 - Service Unavailable), or a controlled error, meaning an error that is related to the parameters' values you send (404 - not found, when no data is found for your query for example). When no transport layer error is returned, you will get a JSON message including the following parameters in the BODY part of the response: Parameter Type Description statusCode number The api call result code (200 if there is no error). statusMessage string The status code related message (optional). info object The requested information. circle-info API RESPONSE EXAMPLE An API call response indicating the job id you can use to fetch results. Copy { "statusCode" : 200 , "info" : { "jobId" : "543433243" } } Previous 'fetch' API Method chevron-left Next Error Status Codes chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago sun-bright desktop moon sun-bright desktop moon

paragraphs: statusCode, statusMessage, info

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right API Endpoint Authentication chevron-right Main API Methods chevron-right API Responses chevron-right Error Status Codes Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down API How Tos chevron-right API Responses Error Status Codes Error Status Codes If an error is detected on our side while running your API call, you will get one of the following error codes: Status Code Meaning Description 400 Bad Request Check your request parameters. 401 Unauthorized Check your API key. 403 Forbidden Forbidden. 404 Not Found No valid API user was found. 405 Method Not Allowed You tried to run an invalid method. 406 Not Acceptable Invalid format. 410 Gone The requested object has been removed. 418 I’m a teapot Yes, I’m a teapot. 429 Too Many Requests Slow down! 500 Unexpected Error Try again later. 503 Service Unavailable Try again later. circle-info API RESPONSE EXAMPLE An API call response indicating a 500 error: Copy { "statusCode" : 500 , "statusMessage" : "Unexpected Error" } Previous API Responses chevron-left Next Certificate Of Trust chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago sun-bright desktop moon sun-bright desktop moon

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right API Endpoint Authentication chevron-right Main API Methods chevron-right 'call' API Method 'fetch' API Method API Responses chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down API How Tos Main API Methods One call to rule them all. Running blockchain solutions is an asynchronous process: You send data to the blockchain and then wait for the network to validate it. circle-exclamation BWS Unified API uses only two methods to call for solution operations: Call and Fetch. The CALL operation starts the blockchain request. FETCH returns the status of the call. Previous Get your API Key chevron-left Next 'call' API Method chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago sun-bright desktop moon sun-bright desktop moon

paragraphs: One call to rule them all.
Running blockchain solutions is an asynchronous process: You send data to the blockchain and then wait for the network to validate it.

BWS Unified API uses only two methods to call for solution operations: Call and Fetch.

The CALL operation starts the blockchain request. FETCH returns the status of the call.

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right API Endpoint Authentication chevron-right Main API Methods chevron-right 'call' API Method 'fetch' API Method API Responses chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down API How Tos chevron-right Main API Methods 'call' API Method POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call BWS Solutions use a single API endpoint to call BWS Solutions: https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call circle-info CODE EXAMPLE The following javascript code shows how to use BWS.Blockchain.Save arrow-up-right to write "Hello World!" to the mumbai blockchain network using the save operation. Copy const response = await fetch ( "https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call" , { method : "post" , body : JSON .stringify ({ solution : "BWS.Blockchain.Save" , version : 1 , network : "mumbai" , operation : "save" , parameters : { value : "Hello World!" , } , }) , headers : { "Content-Type" : "application/json" , "X-Api-Key" : "your api key" , } , }); const result = await response .json (); if ( result .statusCode != 200 ) throw new Error ( result .statusMessage); return result; hashtag Method Parameters Please include the following parameters on each API call in the Body part of a POST request message using JSON arrow-up-right format ( Content-Type header attribute must be set to application/json ) Parameter Type Description solution string The solution id. Each BWS Solution has a unique ID (e.g. BWS.Blockchain.Save) version number The solution version to use. As solutions may get upgraded with new features, it's important to note which version you want to use. network string The network id to use. Each solution will be available on different blockchain networks, just set here which one to use. operation string The operation id to call. Each BWS Solution provides multiple operations to provide a full set of features. parameters json Each operation requires a different set of parameters. Check the operation parameters documentation. Please note: The solution , operation and parameters attributes are used to call any of the Blockchain Web Services available solutions. network is the blockchain network you want to use (for example, use the mumbai network to test without requiring any funds). Check the available networks for the selected solution you plan to use. hashtag API call Response Whenever you execute a call to run a BWS solution, please note the following: You will get a 200 status code if your call is correctly received. If your call is asynchronous, the info response attribute will contain the jobId you can use to call the fetch operation to get the call status and results. If your call is synchronous, the info part will contain the call result values. circle-info API RESPONSE EXAMPLE Response example when running a call to an asynchronous operation. Previous Main API Methods chevron-left Next 'fetch' API Method chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago Method Parameters API call Response sun-bright desktop moon Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "jobId": "aacee908-3a85-4966-945c-ab8f09ebabf9" } } sun-bright desktop moon

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right API Endpoint Authentication chevron-right Main API Methods chevron-right 'call' API Method 'fetch' API Method API Responses chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down API How Tos chevron-right Main API Methods 'fetch' API Method POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/fetch Blockchain operations are mostly asynchronous, meaning you request something and need to wait until the response or confirmation is available. Because of that, you will find most Blockchain Web Services API calls are also asynchronous, meaning you get a jobId you can use it to fetch the operation status and results.\ circle-info CODE EXAMPLE Javascript code example to 'fetch' job status and results. Copy var parameters = { "jobId" : "b064cc6b-f394-4ca4-9c51-be506e4cc59d" , }; $ .ajax ({ method : 'POST' , url : 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/fetch' , dataType : 'json' , data : JSON .stringify (parameters) , headers : { 'Content-Type' : 'application/json' , 'X-Api-Key' : 'ExV0d92KzQ8QgsTVnevddpbB8cUaAfPs7ntVF8g0' } }); hashtag Fetch Use this operation to fetch the status and results of a previously called operation. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/fetch hashtag Request Body Name Type Description parameters * JSON check Method Parameters hashtag Method Parameters Parameter Type Value(s) jobId string The jobId you get when running a call API method . circle-info API RESPONSE EXAMPLE (BWS.NFT.zK) A response showing a job has correctly been registered. hashtag Blockchain Job Status List When calling fetch API endpoint to get a job status, you can get one of the following statuses, representing which status your blockchain process is. Status Description registered The job has correctly been registered for execution. calling The transaction is been called. running The transaction is running on Blockchain Network. transferring In some scenarios, there is a transfer (e.g. transferring an NFT). snapshotting The blockchain transaction has finished, and BWS is creating the Certificate Of Trust (optional). completed Call has completed (success). failed Call execution has failed. pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right Badges User Interface Badges API chevron-right Issuers Badges Awards (Credentials) FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right Blockchain Badges chevron-right Badges API Awards (Credentials) Build or integrate into your solution by using BWS Blockchain Badges API. Awarding a badge represents the digital acknowledgment of an individual's skills, accomplishments, or milestones. This process enables the issuance of verifiable digital credentials to individuals who meet predefined criteria, serving as a modern and secure way to recognize professional development, achievements, or learning milestones. An Open Badge award is a digital credential that adheres to the Open Badge standard, ensuring it includes essential metadata such as the badge name, description, issuing organization, criteria for earning, and the recipient's details. This verifiable and interoperable format allows the awarded badge to be shared across digital platforms, embedded in resumes or social profiles, and trusted as proof of achievement worldwide. hashtag new_award Use this operation to create a new award or credential, with the option to leverage blockchain technology for added transparency and trust. By utilizing blockchain, each award is immutably recorded on a decentralized ledger, ensuring that the credential is tamper-proof and verifiable by anyone, anywhere. This not only enhances the credibility of the award but also provides long-term assurance to recipients and organizations that the achievement cannot be altered or disputed. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.Blockchain.Badges operation * string new_award parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_award Method Parameters circle-info We currently support Matchain blockchain network to certify BWS Blokchain Badge, as the most cost effective solution (1 badge award costs 0.01 USD). Parameter Type Desciption badgeId string The badge ID (check Create a new Badge on how to create a Badge and get its ID). issuedOn long Unix time arrow-up-right in milliseconds representing the date the award was issued. expires long Optional. Unix time arrow-up-right in milliseconds representing the date the award will expire. recipient JSON The recipient fields , including the email and name, representing the identity of the individual being awarded the badge or credential. blockchain string Optional. To enhance the security and verifiability specify the blockchain to use (e.g., "matchain"). Blockchain certification ensures your credentials are tamper-proof, globally verifiable, and maintain long-term trust and transparency. hashtag new_award Recipient Parameters JSON Include the below parameters to identify the certificate (award) recipient. circle-info Recipient email data encryption. When you send the recipient's email, we hash (encrypt) the original, and the readable email address is never saved into our databases. Parameter Type Desciption email string The recipient email address. name string The full name of the recipient. hashtag new_award API Call Example Award a Badge using Node.js hashtag new_award Call Response If everything goes ok, you get the awardId , which is also the URL to access the Open Badge award definition. hashtag list_awards Use this operation to get a list of awards. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.Blockchain.Badges operation * string list_awards parameters JSON optional parameters hashtag list_awards Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption filter JSON The filtering options you want to apply. lastEvaluatedKey string Use lastEvaluatedKey for pagination. hashtag list_awards filter option Parameters circle-exclamation NOTE If you use the filter keyword search option, a maximum of 51 awards will be returned, and no pagination will be available (lastEvaluatedKey). Parameter Type Desciption badgeId * string The badge id you want to filter awards. search string Award recipient search keyword (e.g., Robert). The system will search for any award recipient containing the search text. hashtag list_awards API Call Example Iterate by using lastEvaluatedKey Iterate using badgeId Using recipient To paginate and get new items, use the lastEvaluatedKey you got on the previous call. To list awards linked to a specific badge, use the filter option and badgeId. If pagination is required, use lastEvaluatedKey returned from previous call. To search by recipient name, use the filtering option and set a search keyword. Note that a maximum of 51 items will be returned. hashtag list_awards Call Response Once the API call is executed correctly, you will get a list of badge awards, and if more awards are available, lastEvaluatedKey parameter to use on next call. hashtag send_award_email Use this operation if you want BWS to email an award recipient, including a link to download, share, and visualize his achieved award. Certificate Recipient Email POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.Blockchain.Badges operation * string send_award_email parameters JSON check method parameters hashtag send_award_email Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption awardId string The award ID email string The award recipient's email address hashtag send_award API Call Example circle-exclamation You need to know the award recipient's email address! In full compliance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), our API is designed to be strongly committed to privacy and data protection. We understand the importance of safeguarding personal information and uphold the highest data privacy standards. When our API processes email data, it does so in real time. The data is transmitted securely and never recorded, stored, or saved on our servers. Send Award Email using cURL hashtag send_award_email Call Response You will get a standard 200 response if the email is correctly sent. A5k2V729v', /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, data : JSON.stringify(request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console.log(JSON.stringify(response.data)); }) .catch((error) => { console.log(error); }); Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "awardId": "https://badges.staging.bws.ninja/certify-badge/award/d7da7231-548e-4fc3-b5a9-b0ef2f34e9a8" } } Copy curl paragraphs: Build or integrate into your solution by using BWS Blockchain Badges API.

Awarding a badge represents the digital acknowledgment of an individual's skills, accomplishments, or milestones. This process enables the issuance of verifiable digital credentials to individuals who meet predefined criteria, serving as a modern and secure way to recognize professional development, achievements, or learning milestones.
Use this operation to create a new award or credential, with the option to leverage blockchain technology for added transparency and trust.

By utilizing blockchain, each award is immutably recorded on a decentralized ledger, ensuring that the credential is tamper-proof and verifiable by anyone, anywhere. This not only enhances the credibility of the award but also provides long-term assurance to recipients and organizations that the achievement cannot be altered or disputed.
BWS.Blockchain.Badges
We currently support Matchain blockchain network to certify BWS Blokchain Badge, as the most cost effective solution (1 badge award costs 0.01 USD).

Optional. To enhance the security and verifiability specify the blockchain to use (e.g., "matchain"). Blockchain certification ensures your credentials are tamper-proof, globally verifiable, and maintain long-term trust and transparency.
Include the below parameters to identify the certificate (award) recipient.
When you send the recipient's email, we hash (encrypt) the original, and the readable email address is never saved into our databases.
The recipient email address.
The full name of the recipient.
Use this operation to get a list of awards.
BWS.Blockchain.Badges
The filtering options you want to apply.
Use lastEvaluatedKey for pagination.
If you use the filter keyword search option, a maximum of 51 awards will be returned, and no pagination will be available (lastEvaluatedKey).
The badge id you want to filter awards.
Award recipient search keyword (e.g., Robert). The system will search for any award recipient containing the search text.
To paginate and get new items, use the lastEvaluatedKey you got on the previous call.
To list awards linked to a specific badge, use the filter option and badgeId. If pagination is required, use lastEvaluatedKey returned from previous call.
To search by recipient name, use the filtering option and set a search keyword. Note that a maximum of 51 items will be returned.
Once the API call is executed correctly, you will get a list of badge awards, and if more awards are available, lastEvaluatedKey parameter to use on next call.
Use this operation if you want BWS to email an award recipient, including a link to download, share, and visualize his achieved award.
BWS.Blockchain.Badges
The award recipient's email address

In full compliance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), our API is designed to be strongly committed to privacy and data protection. We understand the importance of safeguarding personal information and uphold the highest data privacy standards.
When our API processes email data, it does so in real time. The data is transmitted securely and never recorded, stored, or saved on our servers.
You will get a standard 200 response if the email is correctly sent.

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right Badges User Interface Badges API chevron-right Issuers Badges Awards (Credentials) FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right Blockchain Badges chevron-right Badges API Badges Build or integrate into your solution by using BWS Blockchain Badges API. A badge is a digital representation of an accomplishment, skill, or authorization that an individual can earn and display. It acts as verifiable proof of the user's achievements and can be shared across various digital platforms, enhancing their professional and personal credibility. In the context of the Open Badge standard, a badge is represented as a JSON object that includes key metadata such as the badge name, description, criteria for earning it, issuing organization, and recipient information. This standardized format ensures interoperability and enables badges to be verified, trusted, and recognized globally. hashtag new_badge Use this operation to create a Badge you can later use to certify recognition (award/credentials). POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.Blockchain.Badges operation * string new_badge parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_badge Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption issuerId string The badge issuer ID (check Add Badge Issuer ) name string Badge name (e.g. "Certified Data Analyst"). description string A description of the badge and what it represents. criteria string Narrative describing the criteria to earn the badge. image base64 The image representing the badge as a base64 encoded string. circle-info Your badge image will be saved on the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) and served from the BWS IPFS gateway service. The images you use should be optimized to be consumed over the internet. You must create your badge image to be less than 1 MB in size for fast access (there is a hard limit of 5MB). Only the PNG image type is accepted. hashtag new_badge API Call Example Create a new Badge using Node.js hashtag new_badge Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the badge id , which is also the url you can use to fetch the badge image. hashtag list_badges Use this operation to get the list of all the badges you created. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.Blockchain.Badges operation * string list_badges hashtag list_badges API Call Example List Badges using cURL hashtag list_badges Call Response Once executed correctly, you will get a list of your badges, including the badge ID and the badge data. hashtag delete_badge Use this API operation to delete an existing Badge. circle-info You can only delete badges with no active Awards! Once a certificate has been awarded to an individual (recipient), the related badge can not be deleted. paragraphs: Build or integrate into your solution by using BWS Blockchain Badges API.

A badge is a digital representation of an accomplishment, skill, or authorization that an individual can earn and display. It acts as verifiable proof of the user's achievements and can be shared across various digital platforms, enhancing their professional and personal credibility.

In the context of the Open Badge standard, a badge is represented as a JSON object that includes key metadata such as the badge name, description, criteria for earning it, issuing organization, and recipient information. This standardized format ensures interoperability and enables badges to be verified, trusted, and recognized globally.
Use this operation to create a Badge you can later use to certify recognition (award/credentials).
BWS.Blockchain.Badges
Badge name (e.g. "Certified Data Analyst").
A description of the badge and what it represents.
Narrative describing the criteria to earn the badge.
The image representing the badge as a base64 encoded string.
You must create your badge image to be less than 1 MB in size for fast access (there is a hard limit of 5MB).
Use this operation to get the list of all the badges you created.
BWS.Blockchain.Badges
Once executed correctly, you will get a list of your badges, including the badge ID and the badge data.
Use this API operation to delete an existing Badge.
Once a certificate has been awarded to an individual (recipient), the related badge can not be deleted.
BWS.Blockchain.Badges
The badge ID to delete.
If the call succeeds and the badge gets deleted, you will get a standard 200 response.

An Issuer represents an entity or organization responsible for defining, creating, and awarding digital badges to acknowledge and verify individual achievements, skills, or competencies. hashtag new_issuer Use this API operation to set up a new Issuer, enabling them to design and issue digital badges and manage awards efficiently. This operation is the first step in establishing a trusted source for verifiable credentials within your platform. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.Blockchain.Badges operation * string new_issuer parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_issuer Method Parameters circle-exclamation Use an email address you have access to! To confirm your authorization to use the provided email, we will send a verification message to it ( How to Verify a Badge Issuer ). Parameter Type Desciption name string Issuer Name (e.g. "Blockchain Academy") url string Issuer website URL email string The issuer contact email address. hashtag new_issuer API Call Example new_issuer API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require ( 'axios' ); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution" : "BWS.Blockchain.Badges" , "operation" : "new_issuer" , "parameters" : { "name" : "Cloud Academy" , "url" : "https://mycloudacademywebsiteurl.com" , "email" : " [email protected] " } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method : 'post' , maxBodyLength : Infinity , url : 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' , headers : { 'X-Api-Key' : 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v' , /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type' : 'application/json' } , data : JSON .stringify (request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console .log ( JSON .stringify ( response .data)); }) .catch((error) => { console .log (error); }); hashtag new_issuer Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the issuerId , which also serves as the URL to retrieve the Issuer's Open Badge v2.0 JSON. This JSON file contains metadata about the Badge Issuer, including essential information such as the organization's name, description, and verification details, formatted according to the Open Badge standard for interoperability and trust. hashtag How to verify a Badge Issuer When you create a new Badge Issuer, we send an email for verification, including a verification link and a verification code. Badge Issuer verification Email example. To verify an Issuer, you - or someone with access to the provided email inbox - should click on the verification link or send you the verification code (once you get the code, use the verify_issuer API call to verify the issuer). circle-exclamation Only verified issuers can create and award badges. Only verified issuers can create and award badges, ensuring high trust and authenticity in the digital credentialing system. hashtag verify_issuer To legitimate badge issuers, we send a verification link and code to the address you use when calling new_issuer API call operation. Use the following operation to verify an issuer using the provided code. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.Blockchain.Badges operation * string verify_issuer parameters * JSON check method parameters 200 Standard 200 response hashtag verify_issuer Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption issuerId string The issuer ID you received when adding a new badge issuer . code string The code you received by email. hashtag verify_issuer API Call Example Verify Issuer using Node.js hashtag verify_issuer Call Response When the API call is successfully executed and the issuer gets verified, you will get a 200 status code. hashtag list_issuers Get the list of the issuers you created. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.Blockchain.Badges operation * string list_issuers hashtag list_issuers API Call Example List Issuers using cURL hashtag list_issuers Call Response Once executed correctly, you will get a list of your account issuers. hashtag delete_issuer Use this API operation to delete an existing Issuer. circle-info You can only delete issuers with no active Badges! Only issuers that have not awarded any badge can be deleted. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.Blockchain.Badges operation * string delete_issuer parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag delete_issuer Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption issuerId string The issuer ID you received when adding a new badge issuer . hashtag delete_issuer API Call Example Delete an Issuer using Node.js hashtag delete_issuer Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, you will get an OK status response code. pages.title: Badges User Interface | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: User interface guide for managing blockchain badges without code. Covers creating issuers, designing badges with templates, awarding certificates to recipients, and searching awards. Explains Open Badge V2.0 standard terminology and email verification process.
useCases: Issue credentials via web dashboard without coding
Design custom badges using built-in template tool
Award certificates with optional blockchain certification
Send automated award emails to recipients
Search and filter awarded badges by recipient name

implementationSteps.title: Create Account and Access

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right Badges User Interface Badges API chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right Blockchain Badges Badges User Interface Easy to use Badges user interface to add issuers, badges and award certificates. Blockchain Badges provides a cutting-edge solution for issuing and managing verifiable digital credentials. Built on the open badge standard and enhanced with blockchain certification, our platform ensures secure, tamper-proof badges that can be trusted worldwide. We offer a robust API for seamless integration into custom systems and a user-friendly interface for companies seeking a more straightforward, no-code solution. Whether through custom integration or an intuitive UI, Blockchain Badges makes credentialing straightforward and accessible for organizations of any size. hashtag How to Start Visit www.bws.ninja, create an account (free), and select 'Blockchain Badges' from the top menu once you're in. hashtag Naming Conventions circle-info Open Badge V2.0 We use the Open Badge arrow-up-right standard, " the world's leading format for digital badges". Open Badges is not a specific product or platform but a type of digital badge that is verifiable, portable, and packed with information about skills and achievements. Let's define the nomenclature you will find in our built-in interface and our solution API . Issuer (Organization, Institution or Individual) An issuer is an entity that provides and awards digital badges to recipients. When a badge is awarded, the issuer's information is encoded within the badge, allowing recipients to showcase their skills or achievements with verifiable proof of where the badge came from and what it represents. Badge (Digital Certificate, Credential) A badge is a digital representation of a skill, achievement, or learning outcome that an individual has acquired. It serves as verifiable, portable digital marks of recognition that can be shared across various platforms and environments. Award (Recognition, Accreditation, Endorsement) An award is a formal recognition of an individual's achievement, skill, or competency, represented by the issue of a digital badge. It validates the recipient's accomplishment and allows them to display and use the badge to symbolize their success and capability. hashtag Manage Issuers Using a simple Issuers list overview, you can Add and Delete badge issuers. Please note that once an Issuer has released an Award, you cannot delete it - badge data should be immutable for trust and transparency. Issuers List To add a new issuer , click on the "+ Badge Issuer" button, inform the Issuer Name, Issuer Email, and Issuer Website, and click the "Add Issuer" button. Information required to define a new Issuer Once registered, your new Issuer will get listed, and you can start creating new badges and reward Digital Certificates. circle-exclamation Email Verification Required When adding a new issuer, the issuer's email will be subject to verification. We send a verification email containing a link and a code to the provided address. The email address owner must follow the instructions (if, for some reason, the email address owner doesn't want to use the verification link, you can ask him to provide the code he receives - you will also be able to verify the issuer using this code). How do you verify an issuer "manually"? If the issuer email owner prefers not to click on links provided in emails, ask him to send you the code included (for example, "CYMDF"). You can then verify the issuer by selecting "Verify Issuer" from the actions menu on the list. Verify an Issuer using the code hashtag Define new Badges Once you've set up the Badge Issuers you manage, you can begin creating and customizing the badges you'll award to your alumni, team, or other recipients. Our intuitive badge designer tool makes this process simple and flexible. Start with a template, add images and text, and customize colors to reflect your branding or the recognized achievement. Designing a badge has never been easier! As for the Open Badge specification, a badge consists of an image, a name, a description, and the criteria to be awarded. circle-info Criteria Text Formatting While there is no standard for the criteria format, and plain text should be used, we recommend that you write a line per each criterion required to get awarded. We will format each of the lines as in the example below. hashtag Reward a Badge To create a new award, start by selecting the Issuer and the certificate you wish to award to an individual. Then, click the "+ Award" button. Enter the Certificate name, recipient’s email, and validity period. You can also choose from two optional features to enhance the award process: Send Award Email : This option sends an email to the recipient with instructions on downloading and sharing their badge, ensuring a smooth and seamless experience. Blockchain Certification : By enabling this option, the badge is certified on the blockchain, adding an extra layer of security and verifiability. Blockchain certification ensures the badge is tamper-proof and traceable, providing long-term credibility and trust for both the issuer and the recipient. circle-info To use blockchain certification, you need a Professional Plan. The cost per award will be approximately 0.01 USD. Once all details are set, click the "Award Badge" button to finalize the process. triangle-exclamation Important Note on Data Confidentiality. In full compliance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), our API is designed to be firmly committed to privacy and data protection. We understand the importance of safeguarding personal information and uphold the highest data privacy standards. Our API processes email data in real-time. The data is transmitted securely and never recorded, stored, or saved on our servers. If you check "send download instructions," you will receive an email with instructions on downloading, viewing, or sharing the recipient's new award. If you decide not to send the instructions when creating a new certificate, you can still send them later. However, you must inform the recipient's email address to do so. hashtag Award Email When a new award is created, you can email the recipient clear instructions on downloading and sharing their newly earned achievement. The email template below will notify the recipient if you send download instructions. Download Instructions hashtag Search Awards You can use the recipient's name to search for awarded certificates. Go to the Award Badges menu option, select the badge you awarded the recipient, and use the search box to filter the results. paragraphs: Easy to use Badges user interface to add issuers, badges and award certificates.

Blockchain Badges provides a cutting-edge solution for issuing and managing verifiable digital credentials. Built on the open badge standard and enhanced with blockchain certification, our platform ensures secure, tamper-proof badges that can be trusted worldwide.

We offer a robust API for seamless integration into custom systems and a user-friendly interface for companies seeking a more straightforward, no-code solution. Whether through custom integration or an intuitive UI, Blockchain Badges makes credentialing straightforward and accessible for organizations of any size.
Visit www.bws.ninja, create an account (free), and select 'Blockchain Badges' from the top menu once you're in.
An issuer is an entity that provides and awards digital badges to recipients.

When a badge is awarded, the issuer's information is encoded within the badge, allowing recipients to showcase their skills or achievements with verifiable proof of where the badge came from and what it represents.

A badge is a digital representation of a skill, achievement, or learning outcome that an individual has acquired. It serves as verifiable, portable digital marks of recognition that can be shared across various platforms and environments.

An award is a formal recognition of an individual's achievement, skill, or competency, represented by the issue of a digital badge. It validates the recipient's accomplishment and allows them to display and use the badge to symbolize their success and capability.

Using a simple Issuers list overview, you can Add and Delete badge issuers. Please note that once an Issuer has released an Award, you cannot delete it - badge data should be immutable for trust and transparency.
Once registered, your new Issuer will get listed, and you can start creating new badges and reward Digital Certificates.
When adding a new issuer, the issuer's email will be subject to verification. We send a verification email containing a link and a code to the provided address.

The email address owner must follow the instructions (if, for some reason, the email address owner doesn't want to use the verification link, you can ask him to provide the code he receives - you will also be able to verify the issuer using this code).

If the issuer email owner prefers not to click on links provided in emails, ask him to send you the code included (for example, "CYMDF"). You can then verify the issuer by selecting "Verify Issuer" from the actions menu on the list.

Once you've set up the Badge Issuers you manage, you can begin creating and customizing the badges you'll award to your alumni, team, or other recipients. Our intuitive badge designer tool makes this process simple and flexible. Start with a template, add images and text, and customize colors to reflect your branding or the recognized achievement. Designing a badge has never been easier!
As for the Open Badge specification, a badge consists of an image, a name, a description, and the criteria to be awarded.

While there is no standard for the criteria format, and plain text should be used, we recommend that you write a line per each criterion required to get awarded.

We will format each of the lines as in the example below.
To use blockchain certification, you need a Professional Plan. The cost per award will be approximately 0.01 USD.

In full compliance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), our API is designed to be firmly committed to privacy and data protection. We understand the importance of safeguarding personal information and uphold the highest data privacy standards.
Our API processes email data in real-time. The data is transmitted securely and never recorded, stored, or saved on our servers.
If you decide not to send the instructions when creating a new certificate, you can still send them later. However, you must inform the recipient's email address to do so.

When a new award is created, you can email the recipient clear instructions on downloading and sharing their newly earned achievement. The email template below will notify the recipient if you send download instructions.

It serves banks, investment advisors, institutional investors, venture capitalists, and issuers of green assets by enabling the creation of detailed ESG impact reports for various stakeholders. Certification bodies can also use the API to publish quality-assured impact data. The solution supports ICMA frameworks and custom taxonomies, allows flexible impact calculations, and integrates seamlessly with blockchain for secure, immutable storage and certification of green investments, preventing double-counting and ensuring transparency. paragraphs: For financial institutions that want to provide positive ESG impact reporting in their digital channels and investment reporting

It serves banks, investment advisors, institutional investors, venture capitalists, and issuers of green assets by enabling the creation of detailed ESG impact reports for various stakeholders. Certification bodies can also use the API to publish quality-assured impact data.

The solution supports ICMA frameworks and custom taxonomies, allows flexible impact calculations, and integrates seamlessly with blockchain for secure, immutable storage and certification of green investments, preventing double-counting and ensuring transparency.

pages.title: ESG Credits API | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API architecture overview for ESG Credits, organizing operations into sections: Taxonomy structure and SDG connections, Translations for multilingual support, Currencies and FX rates, Frameworks for green projects, Issuers and Assets management, Investors and Positions tracking, Portfolios and Impacts calculations, Blockchain certification, and Users management.
useCases: Build custom ESG impact calculation engines
Create multilingual ESG reporting platforms
Track green project benefits across portfolios
Calculate environmental impact from investments
Certify ESG impacts on blockchain for transparency

implementationSteps.title: Set Up Taxonomy
implementationSteps.description: Define taxonomy structure with categories, sub-categories, and indicators linked to UN SDGs

implementationSteps.title: Configure Frameworks and Assets
implementationSteps.description: Input frameworks with benefit characteristics and register green assets linked to them

implementationSteps.title: Register Investors and Positions
implementationSteps.description: Add investors and their positions in green assets for impact calculations

implementationSteps.title: Calculate and Certify Impacts
implementationSteps.description: Use impact operations to calculate benefits and optionally certify on blockchain

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right Solution Overview ESG Credits API chevron-right Taxonomy Translations Currencies Frameworks Projects Issuers Assets Investors Positions Portfolios Impacts Blockchain Users X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right ESG Credits ESG Credits API Available operations to set up and run your ESG impact services are divided into different sections. Under section "Taxonomy" you build your taxonomy structure including connections to UN SDGs and EU Environmental Development Objectives. Taxonomies can be set up with categories and sub categories as well as indicators and super indicators, where super indicators are linked to categories as per the ICMA logic. Taxonomies can be copied across to the following year if the taxonomy is identical or similar. The section "Translations" contains operations to add taxonomy phrases in additional languages, making your ESG impact services multilingual, with alternative languages accessed easily through a simple parameter in your API calls. Under section "Currencies" you can define the currencies that your solution will support. Foreign exchange rates for supported currencies can be set on annual basis (typically average rates). In the section called "Frameworks", you input the category volumes and indicators values which build up your green framework based on your taxonomy. This dataset is consequently used for ESG impact benefit calculations throughout the solution. Frameworks can be made private or public, where in the latter case other tenants using ESG.Credits can access such frameworks. Under section " Projects " you find operations to register green projects and their benefit characteristics in terms of category volumes and indicator values as per your taxonomy. Benefit characteristics of a portfolio of green projects can be consolidataed into a framework. Projects can be made private or public, where in the latter case other tenants using ESG.Credits can access such projects. Before adding green assets for your customers / investors to see impacts from, you first need to enter the issuers, which is done under section "Issuers" . This can be your own franchise or one whose green assets you are re-selling. Issuers can be made private or public, where in the latter case other tenants using ESG.Credits can access such issuers. Under section "Assets" , you add the green assets in scope, and connect them to the relevant framework for ESG impact calculation possibilities. Assets can be made private or public, where in the latter case other tenants using ESG.Credits can access such assets, and also calculate ESG impact benefits for investments in them. The section "Investors" has operations for adding your customers / investors to the ESG.Credits API solution. This includes encryption operations for personal identifiable information. Note: It is possible to make ESG impact calculations without storing customer records in ESG.Credits, why this step can be omitted. Before actual ESG impact calculations can be made for registered customers / investors, the position taken, or investment made, in a green assets must be registered. This is done under section "Positions" . Here you would also specify the term of the position, which also impacts the impact level. In section "Portfolio" you can cluster positions into different portfolios, so that the impact can be calculated for each portfolio as a whole. Under section "Impacts" you find operations for calculating the benefit impact from a particular position, investor, or portfolio. Operations can also support the use case where the investors are not stored in ESG.Credits, when it is only used as a calculation engine. The "Blockchain" section has operations to write and read from the blockchain. This could for instance be certificates of ESG benefit impacts for increased trust and avoidance of impact benefit claim duplication. The blockchain can also be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community. Finally, the "Users" section. Here you find operations for to manage the end users of your client account at BWS.ESG.Credits. It includes the possibility to connect end users to particular investors that are in scope for each user. You can also tie users to certain user groups which you can use to control availability of functionality in your user interface. Previous Solution Overview chevron-left Next Taxonomy chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago sun-bright desktop moon sun-bright desktop moon

pages.title: Assets | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API documentation for managing green assets in ESG Credits. Includes operations for retrieving asset information with filtering, getting framework details for specific assets, adding or updating assets with issuer and framework links, and removing assets from the repository.

codeExamples.description: Retrieve green assets filtered by issuer and framework visibility
Get framework details and impact indicators for a specific asset
Add a new green asset with issuer, framework, and maturity information

useCases: Register green bonds in ESG reporting system
Link green assets to environmental frameworks
Retrieve framework details for impact calculations
Manage green asset portfolios with maturity dates
Remove assets when positions no longer exist

implementationSteps.title: Add Green Asset
implementationSteps.description: Call addAsset with ISIN, issuer, framework identifier, value, currency, and dates

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Asset Details
implementationSteps.description: Use getAssets to list assets or getAssetFrameworkDetails for specific framework information

implementationSteps.title: Manage Asset Lifecycle
implementationSteps.description: Update assets by calling addAsset again or remove with removeAsset when no positions exist

pages.title: Blockchain | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents blockchain operations for ESG Credits API, including reading job status from blockchain and writing green bond frameworks and position impacts to blockchain in both HTML and JSON formats. Supports both permanent storage and retrieval of ESG impact data on blockchain networks.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves the status of a blockchain entry using a job ID to track the progress of blockchain write operations

useCases: Write green bond frameworks to blockchain for increased commitment and trust
Store position impact benefits on blockchain to generate offset certificates
Retrieve framework data from blockchain for transparency reporting
Track blockchain job status for ESG data writes
Create immutable records of environmental impact investments

implementationSteps.title: Prepare Framework Data
implementationSteps.description: Collect framework identifier, reporting year, and specify output format (HTML or JSON) along with optional parameters like SDG goals and EU objectives inclusion

implementationSteps.title: Write to Blockchain
implementationSteps.description: Call writeHtmlFrameworkToBlockchain or writeJsonFrameworkToBlockchain with solution, operation, and network parameters to store data immutably

implementationSteps.title: Monitor Job Status
implementationSteps.description: Use getJobStatusFromBlockchain with the returned job_id to track the progress and completion of the blockchain write operation

pages.title: Currencies | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Provides API operations for managing currencies and foreign exchange rates within ESG Credits. Enables users to register currencies, add FX rates for specific reporting years, and remove currency data to support multi-currency green asset portfolios and impact calculations.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all registered currencies for the account, returning currency codes and IDs
Adds a foreign exchange rate between two currencies for a specific reporting year

useCases: Register currencies applicable to green asset universe
Manage foreign exchange rates for cross-border green investments
Convert ESG impact values across different currencies
Support multi-currency green bond portfolios
Enable accurate reporting for international ESG investments

implementationSteps.title: Add Currency Codes
implementationSteps.description: Use addCurrency operation to register standard 3-character currency codes (EUR, USD, SEK, etc.) that will be used in green asset transactions

implementationSteps.title: Register FX Rates
implementationSteps.description: Add foreign exchange rates between currency pairs using addFXrate with numerator, denominator, quota, and reporting year

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Currency Data
implementationSteps.description: Use getCurrencies and getFXrates operations to list available currencies and exchange rates for calculations

pages.title: Frameworks | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents API operations for creating and managing green frameworks, including green bond frameworks. Supports adding framework details, year-specific metrics, category indicators, and removal operations. Enables linking ESG impact categories with funding allocation data.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all private or public green frameworks for a specific reporting year

useCases: Create green bond frameworks with ESG impact categories
Track use of proceeds allocation across environmental projects
Register framework-level ESG benefits and indicators
Manage year-specific framework details and funding amounts
Build frameworks with visibility controls (public or private)

implementationSteps.title: Create Framework
implementationSteps.description: Use addFramework to register framework identifier, name, currency code, and visibility setting

implementationSteps.title: Add Year Specifics
implementationSteps.description: Call addYearSpecificsToFramework with value outstanding, allocated amount, and reporting year data

implementationSteps.title: Register Impact Categories
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategoryIndicatorToFramework to link sub-categories, indicators, and ESG benefit values to the framework

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Framework Data
implementationSteps.description: Query frameworks using getFrameworks and getFrameworkDetails for reporting and analysis

pages.title: Impacts | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Details operations for calculating and retrieving ESG impact data from green asset investments. Supports position-level, investor-level, and portfolio-level impact calculations with options for PDF report generation. Enables filtering by categories, indicators, and language preferences.

codeExamples.description: Calculates and stores ESG impacts for a registered investment position based on amount, currency, and investment term

useCases: Calculate ESG benefits from green bond investments
Generate investor impact reports with carbon emission reductions
Consolidate portfolio-level environmental impacts
Track renewable energy contributions from investments
Create certificates for carbon offset documentation

implementationSteps.title: Calculate Position Impacts
implementationSteps.description: Use calculatePositionImpacts or calculatePositionImpactsOnly with position amount, currency, days, and reporting year to compute ESG benefits

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Impact Data
implementationSteps.description: Call getPositionImpacts, getInvestorImpacts, or getPortfolioImpacts to retrieve calculated impacts with optional filtering by category or indicator

implementationSteps.title: Generate Reports
implementationSteps.description: Specify report_template and report_name parameters to generate PDF investor reports with ESG impact metrics

paragraphs: This section deals with the impact from the green assets, as per the particular investments made in them. Any impact data is removed when the associated position is removed.

Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular position. The operation can return either a JSON formatted data set, or PDF file based on your template which you can provide to your customer as an investor report.
check Operation parameters
integer identifier for a investment
For decrypting personally identifiable information
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.
Input report template name
Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular investor. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are stacked on each other.
check Operation parameters
String identifier for an investor
For a specified asset of an investor
For decrypting personally identifiable information
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.
Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular portfolio. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are consolidated when possibel (when the green framworks are the same).
check Operation parameters
String identifier for a portfolio
For a specified asset in a portfolio
For decrypting personally identifiable information
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.
Use this operation to calculate the ESG impacts for a particular position already registered in ESG.Credits.
calculatePositionImpacts
check Operation parameters
Integer identifier for a position
Amount of money invested
position_currency_code
Currency code of the investment, as per getCurrencies
The term of the investment, default is 365 days

Use this operation to calculate the ESG impacts for a position without any data is stored or logged in ESG.Credits. This operation is applicable for cases when financial institutes wants to utilise ESG.Credits only for calculations and store and investor data in own data repositories.
calculatePositionImpactsOnly
check Operation parameters
String identifier for an asset
Amount of money invested
position_currency_code
Currency code of the investment, as per getCurrencies
The term of the investment, default is 365 days
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.

pages.title: Investors | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Covers investor management operations including creating, retrieving, encrypting, and removing investor records. Supports personally identifiable information (PII) encryption for data privacy and enables investor identifier generation for anonymization purposes.

codeExamples.description: Creates or updates investor information with optional PII encryption using an encryption key
Generates a unique encryption key for encrypting and decrypting personally identifiable information

useCases: Register green bond investors with encrypted personal data
Generate anonymous investor identifiers for privacy compliance
Manage investor portfolios with PII protection
Enable investor correspondence for ESG impact reporting
Remove investor records while maintaining data integrity

implementationSteps.title: Generate Encryption Key
implementationSteps.description: Use generateEncryptionKey to create a secure key for PII encryption and store it safely for future decryption needs

implementationSteps.title: Create Investor Record
implementationSteps.description: Call addInvestor with investor identifier, name, currency code, email, and optional encryption key to register investor

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Investor Data
implementationSteps.description: Use getInvestors with optional decryption_key parameter to access investor information with decrypted PII

pages.title: Issuers | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents operations for managing issuers of green assets, including creating, retrieving, and removing issuer records. Supports public and private visibility settings for cross-account issuer data sharing and manages issuer currency configurations.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all public or private issuers of green assets from the ESG Credits repository
Creates or updates issuer information with visibility controls and currency settings

useCases: Register banks and financial institutions issuing green bonds
Manage issuer visibility for public or private sharing
Track issuers of environmental project financing
Update issuer information and currency preferences
Remove issuers from ESG Credits repository

implementationSteps.title: Add Issuer
implementationSteps.description: Use addIssuer with issuer identifier, name, currency code, and visibility setting (public or private)

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Issuers
implementationSteps.description: Call getIssuers with optional visibility filter to list available green asset issuers

implementationSteps.title: Remove Issuer
implementationSteps.description: Use removeIssuer with issuer identifier to delete issuer records (only if no associated assets exist)

This operation also alters already existing issuer information. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addIssuer parameters * JSON check Operation parameters hashtag Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message hashtag Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description issuer_identifier string yes String identifier for an issuer issuer_name string yes Issuer name issuer_currency_code string yes Currency code of the asset, as per getCurrencies issuer_visibility string no Public or private. Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts. hashtag Example code curl hashtag removeIssuer hashtag Removes an issuer POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to remove issuers of green assets from the ESG.Credits data repository. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string removeIssuer parameters * JSON check Operation parameters hashtag Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message hashtag Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description issuer_identifier string yes String identifier for an issuer hashtag Example code curl Previous Projects chevron-left Next Assets chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago getIssuers Returns private or public issuers addIssuer Creates or updates information about an existing issuer removeIssuer Removes an issuer sun-bright desktop moon Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.ESG.Credits", "operation": "getIssuers", "parameters": { "issuer_visibility": "public" } } Copy { "message": "Issuer was successfully added." } Copy { "message": "Invalid issuer visibility term used." } Copy { "message": "Not authorized." } Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.ESG.Credits", "operation": "addIssuer", "parameters": { "issuer_identifier": "good_issuer", "issuer_name": "Good Bank", "issuer_currency_code": "EUR", "issuer_visibility": "private" } } Copy { "Issuer was successfully removed." } Copy { "message": "Issuer was not removed. pages.title: Portfolios | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Provides operations for managing investor portfolios of green assets, including creating portfolios, adding positions to portfolios, and retrieving portfolio-level investment data. Enables portfolio-based impact consolidation and investor asset grouping.

codeExamples.description: Creates or updates a portfolio for an investor to group green asset investments
Adds an investment position to a specific investor portfolio for consolidated impact reporting

useCases: Create green investment portfolios for individual investors
Group multiple green bond investments into portfolios
Track portfolio-level ESG impacts across multiple assets
Manage investor asset allocations by portfolio
Remove positions from portfolios before investment deletion

implementationSteps.title: Create Portfolio
implementationSteps.description: Use addPortfolio with investor identifier and portfolio identifier to establish a portfolio container

implementationSteps.title: Add Positions
implementationSteps.description: Call addPositionToPortfolio to associate specific investment positions with the portfolio

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Portfolio Data
implementationSteps.description: Use getPortfolios and getPortfolioPositions to view portfolio contents and calculate consolidated impacts

pages.title: Positions | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents operations for managing investment positions in green assets, including creating, retrieving, and removing positions. Supports position-specific data like investment amount, currency, term length, and automatic impact calculation upon position creation.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all investment positions for a specific investor with optional filtering by asset and issuer
Creates or updates an investment position with amount, currency, term, and optional impact calculation

useCases: Register investor purchases of green bonds
Track investment positions across multiple green assets
Calculate ESG impacts automatically upon position creation
Manage position data including amount, currency, and investment term
Remove positions and associated impact records

implementationSteps.title: Create Position
implementationSteps.description: Use addPosition with investor identifier, asset identifier, position amount, currency code, and optional reporting year for automatic impact calculation

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Positions
implementationSteps.description: Call getPositions with investor identifier and optional filters to view all investments

implementationSteps.title: Remove Position
implementationSteps.description: Use removePosition with position ID to delete investment record and associated ESG impact data

pages.title: Projects | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Covers operations for managing green projects and building frameworks from project consolidation. Includes adding project details, year-specific metrics, category indicators, and consolidating multiple projects into unified frameworks for green bond reporting.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all public or private green projects for a specific reporting year
Consolidates impact data from multiple projects into a unified green bond framework

useCases: Register specific renewable energy projects like wind farms and solar installations
Track allocated funding amounts to individual green projects
Build green bond frameworks by consolidating project impacts
Manage project-level ESG indicators and benefits
Report use of proceeds at project granularity

implementationSteps.title: Create Projects
implementationSteps.description: Use addProject to register project identifier, name, currency code, linked framework, and visibility settings

implementationSteps.title: Add Project Details
implementationSteps.description: Call addYearSpecificsToProject and addCategoryIndicatorToProject to register allocated amounts and ESG indicators

implementationSteps.title: Consolidate to Framework
implementationSteps.description: Use buildFrameworkFromProjects to aggregate project impacts into a unified framework for reporting

paragraphs: This section has operations to register projects and their impact characteristics, and one operation to consolidate projects into a green framework
Use this operation to retrieve green projects registered in ESG.Credits data repository.
check Operation parameters
Public or private. Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts.
Use this operations to retrieve green project details.
check Operation parameters
String identifier for a project
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.
Use this operation to add green projects to the ESG.Credits data repository.
check Operation parameters
String identifier for a project
project_currency_code
Currency code of the project, as per getCurrencies
String identifier for the framework to be built
Public or private. Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts.
Use this operation to set up the green project with details that are year specific.
addYearSpecificsToProject
check Operation parameters
String identifier for a project
Amount of funding allocated to the project for the reporting year of the framework, for the corresponding impacts registered
Use this operation to set up the different impact categories, indicators, and units, with specified values.
addCategoryIndicatorToProject
check Operation parameters
String identifier for a project
Integer identifier of a sub category
Amount of funding / use of proceeds
Integer identifier of an indicator
ESG benefit value, in the unit specified by the taxonomy

Use this operation to consolidate project impact data into a green framework, as an option to register the framework as a whole. The framework needs to be created and year specifics added. Projects need to refer to the framework.
buildFrameworkFromProjects
check Operation parameters
String identifier for a framework to be built from consolidation of associated projects
