pages.title: Taxonomy | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents taxonomy operations including UN Sustainable Development Goals, EU Environmental Objectives, and custom taxonomy elements like categories, sub-categories, indicators, and units. Supports building custom ESG measurement frameworks with multilingual support.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all 17 United Nations Sustainable Development Goals available for taxonomy referencing
Adds a new ESG category to the taxonomy for a specific reporting year

useCases: Build custom ESG taxonomies aligned with UN SDGs
Reference EU Environmental Objectives in impact reporting
Create categories like renewable energy and energy efficiency
Define indicators such as CO2 emission reduction and energy production
Link taxonomy elements to international sustainability standards

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Reference Standards
implementationSteps.description: Use getSDGs and getEUObjectives to explore available UN and EU sustainability frameworks for taxonomy alignment

implementationSteps.title: Build Taxonomy Structure
implementationSteps.description: Call addCategory, addSubCategory, addSuperIndicator, and addIndicator to create hierarchical taxonomy elements

implementationSteps.title: Link Standards
implementationSteps.description: Use addGoalObjectiveToSubCategory to associate UN SDGs and EU Objectives with taxonomy sub-categories

pages.title: Translations | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing multilingual translations of taxonomy elements in ESG Credits. Covers operations to retrieve available languages and add translations for categories, sub-categories, super-indicators, indicators, and units across different reporting years.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all registered languages available for taxonomy translations
Adds a Swedish language translation to a taxonomy category for 2023 reporting year
Example response showing available language codes and names

useCases: Make ESG taxonomy multilingual for international reporting
Translate environmental impact categories into local languages
Localize sustainability indicators for regional compliance
Support multi-language ESG reporting frameworks
Enable translation of measurement units across languages

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Available Languages
implementationSteps.description: Call getLanguages operation to get list of supported language codes (en, sv, dk, etc.) for your translations

implementationSteps.title: Add Translations for Taxonomy Elements
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategoryTranslation, addIndicatorTranslation, or addUnitTranslation operations with category_id, reporting_year, language_code, and translation text

implementationSteps.title: Update or Change Translations
implementationSteps.description: Re-run the same add operation with new translation text to update existing translations for specific taxonomy items

languages for when you are to register translations for categories, sub-categories, super-indicators, indicators, and units, when building up your taxonomy. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description "language_code" : "en" , "language" : "English" } , { "id" : 2 , "language_code" : "sv" , "language" : "Svenska" } , { "id" : 3 , "language_code" : "dk" , "language" : "Dansk" } , { "id" : 4 , "language_code" : "pl" , "language" : "Polskie" } , { "id" : 8 , "language_code" : "fr" , "language" : "Français" } , { "id" : 9 , "language_code" : "nl" , "language" : "Nederlands" } , { "id" : 10 , "language_code" : "es" , "language" : "Español" } , { "id" : 11 , "language_code" : "pt" , "language" : "Portoguêse" } , { "id" : 12 , "language_code" : "it" , "language" : "Italiano" } ] hashtag implementationSteps.title: Create User Account
implementationSteps.description: Use addUser operation with user_identifier, name, email, role (user or admin), and optional encryption_key for sensitive data

implementationSteps.title: Assign User to Groups

implementationSteps.title: Grant Investor Access
implementationSteps.description: Use addInvestorToUser operation to give specific users access to particular investor's portfolio and impact data

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Overview of the ESG Credits marketplace solution for financial institutions. Explains how banks, investors, and venture capitalists can add ESG impact reporting to digital channels, calculate green asset benefits, and issue blockchain certificates for sustainable investments.
useCases: Banks creating ESG investor reports for customer digital channels
Institutional investors viewing portfolio reports for green assets
Venture capitalists providing sponsors with project-level impact reports
Issuers publishing impact details of green assets for promotion
Certification bodies digitally publishing quality-assured project impacts

implementationSteps.title: Define Taxonomy and Framework
implementationSteps.description: Set up your ESG taxonomy with impact categories, indicators, and units following ICMA or custom framework

implementationSteps.title: Register Green Projects and Assets
implementationSteps.description: Add green project benefits and frameworks with impact profiles linked to UN SDGs or EU Environmental Objectives

implementationSteps.title: Calculate and Report Impact
implementationSteps.description: Generate consolidated impact reports for investor portfolios with calculations per annum or instrument term

implementationSteps.title: Issue Blockchain Certificates
implementationSteps.description: Store ESG impact profiles immutably on blockchain and issue investment certificates to demonstrate commitment

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right Solution Overview ESG Credits API chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right ESG Credits Solution Overview BWS.ESG.Credits solution overview This API solution is designed for financial institutions that want to quickly add ESG impact benefits to their digital channels and investment reporting. The benefits come from investments made in green assets. Typical use cases include: Banks and investment advisors who want to create and provide investor reports to their broad customer base in digital channels, for the specific scope of green assets they market. Institutional investors in green assets who want to create and view investment reports for their own investment portfolio of broadly available green assets. Venture capitalists funding green projects that want to create and provide their sponsors with investor reports on both project and consolidated levels, for each sponsor’s investment mix. Issuers of green assets that want to provide banks, advisors, and investors with impact details for promotional purposes, and for use in impact calculations. Certification bodies and agents that want to digitally publish quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects and green frameworks to issuers of green assets. By utilizing this API solution, your development team can accelerate the development of your ESG impact reporting solution. The underlying information model and calculation methods are designed to make it easy for you to add impact reporting to your digital channels and investment reporting. Impact reporting can be done as per the ICMA framework or any taxonomy of choice. The solution supports two levels of impact categories and two levels of impact indicators, as well as any impact unit. Impact amounts are calculated per annum or as per the term of the financial instrument. The green frameworks' “impact profile” forms the basis for calculations. Benefit characteristics of the green projects invested in can be consolidated and form your green framework. You can tie United Nations SDGs and EU’s Environmental Objectives classifications to taxonomy impact categories as per your choice. Any combination of currencies are supported, and your choice of taxonomy language can be accessed interchangeable. The ESG impact profile of your framework can be immutably stored in a blockchain to demonstrate your committment. Blockchain certificates for your customers' specific investment in green assets can also be issued with help of ESG.Credits. Finally, the blockchain can be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community. Previous ESG Credits chevron-left Next ESG Credits API chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago sun-bright desktop moon sun-bright desktop moon

paragraphs: BWS.ESG.Credits solution overview

This API solution is designed for financial institutions that want to quickly add ESG impact benefits to their digital channels and investment reporting. The benefits come from investments made in green assets. Typical use cases include:
Banks and investment advisors who want to create and provide investor reports to their broad customer base in digital channels, for the specific scope of green assets they market.
Institutional investors in green assets who want to create and view investment reports for their own investment portfolio of broadly available green assets.
Venture capitalists funding green projects that want to create and provide their sponsors with investor reports on both project and consolidated levels, for each sponsor’s investment mix.
Issuers of green assets that want to provide banks, advisors, and investors with impact details for promotional purposes, and for use in impact calculations.
Certification bodies and agents that want to digitally publish quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects and green frameworks to issuers of green assets.

By utilizing this API solution, your development team can accelerate the development of your ESG impact reporting solution. The underlying information model and calculation methods are designed to make it easy for you to add impact reporting to your digital channels and investment reporting.

Impact reporting can be done as per the ICMA framework or any taxonomy of choice. The solution supports two levels of impact categories and two levels of impact indicators, as well as any impact unit.

Impact amounts are calculated per annum or as per the term of the financial instrument. The green frameworks' “impact profile” forms the basis for calculations. Benefit characteristics of the green projects invested in can be consolidated and form your green framework.

You can tie United Nations SDGs and EU’s Environmental Objectives classifications to taxonomy impact categories as per your choice. Any combination of currencies are supported, and your choice of taxonomy language can be accessed interchangeable.

The ESG impact profile of your framework can be immutably stored in a blockchain to demonstrate your committment. Blockchain certificates for your customers' specific investment in green assets can also be issued with help of ESG.Credits. Finally, the blockchain can be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community.

pages.title: Calendar | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API operations for managing Calendar objects representing scheduled games, matches, or tournaments. Calendar anchors the gameplay timeline and links time-bound events to specific Fields, supporting season-based or single-event campaigns.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new calendar for the 2025-26 season using Node.js
Successful response returning the unique calendar ID

useCases: Create UEFA Champions League Final calendar for single match
Set up LaLiga 2025-2026 season calendar with multiple matches
Schedule tournament instances for fan gameplay campaigns
Manage time-bound sports events linked to specific fields
List and delete calendar objects for season management

implementationSteps.title: Create Calendar
implementationSteps.description: Call new_calendar operation with name parameter (e.g., '2025-26') to create a time-bound event container

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Calendar ID
implementationSteps.description: Store the returned calendarId from the response to link matches and gameplay events

implementationSteps.title: List or Delete Calendars
implementationSteps.description: Use list_calendars to view all calendars or delete_calendar with calendarId to remove scheduled events

The Calendar object anchors the gameplay timeline, enabling season-based, game-based, or tournament-based fan campaigns. Examples: UEFA Champions League Final : Madrid vs PSG, for a single event calendar. LaLiga 2025-2026 , including multiple matches, for the entire season calendar. hashtag new_calendar POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Calendar name (e.g. LaLiga 2025-26) hashtag new_calendar API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require ( 'axios' ); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution" : "BWS.NFT.GameCube" , "operation" : "new_calendar" , "parameters" : { "name" : "2025-26" } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method : 'post' , maxBodyLength : Infinity , url : 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' , headers : { 'X-Api-Key' : 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v' , /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type' : 'application/json' } , data : JSON .stringify (request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console .log ( JSON .stringify ( response .data)); }) .catch((error) => { console .log (error); }); hashtag new_calendar Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the calendarId , which will be used to link with related objects. hashtag list_calendars POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_fields parameters JSON (optional) check method parameters hashtag list_calendars Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption calendar_id string Calendar Id hashtag list_calendars API Call Example list_calendars API call using Node.js hashtag list_calendars Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the list of calendars you created or a single calendar if the CalendarId parameter is included in the call. hashtag delete_calendar POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag delete_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption calendarId string Then Calendar Id of the calendar to delete. hashtag delete_calendar API Call Example delete_calendar API call using Node.js hashtag delete_calendar Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Cubes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API operations for managing Cubes, which are NFT zones or tiles within a Field. Each Cube represents a unique tokenized section owned by fans, tracks ownership, enables rewards, and reflects live event impacts during matches.

codeExamples.description: Lists up to 5 cubes from a field with pagination support
Example cube object with coordinates, dimensions, and status

useCases: List all NFT cubes available in a sports field
Update cube prices for marketplace sales
Track cube ownership by fans for reward distribution
Map live match events to specific cube coordinates
Manage hundreds of field sections with pagination

implementationSteps.title: Understand Cube Auto-Generation
implementationSteps.description: Cubes are automatically created when you create a new field, dividing it into NFT zones based on cubeLength parameter

implementationSteps.title: List Cubes with Pagination
implementationSteps.description: Use list_cubes with fieldId and limit parameters, include lastEvaluatedKey in subsequent calls for next chunk

implementationSteps.title: Update Cube Pricing
implementationSteps.description: Call update_cube-price with fieldId, cubesList (array of i_j indexes), and priceInCents to set USD pricing

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API chevron-right Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Cube API Cubes Represents a zone or tile within a Field . Each Cube is a unique NFT owned by a fan. It tracks ownership, enables rewards, and reflects live event impacts during matches. triangle-exclamation Cubes are generated automatically when creating a new field. hashtag list_cubes POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_cubes parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id. limit number The maximum number of cubes returned (a field can have hundreds of cubes). lastEvaluatedKey (optional) If more cubes are available, include the returned key to get the next chink of cubes. hashtag list_cubes API Call Example list_cubes API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require ( 'axios' ); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution" : "BWS.NFT.GameCube" , "operation" : "list_cubes" , "parameters" : { "fieldId" : "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2" , "limit" : 5 } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method : 'post' , maxBodyLength : Infinity , url : 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' , headers : { 'X-Api-Key' : 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v' , /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type' : 'application/json' } , data : JSON .stringify (request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console .log ( JSON .stringify ( response .data)); }) .catch((error) => { console .log (error); }); hashtag list_cubes Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns a list of cubes and their related cube data, including the cubeId . If more cubes are available, the field lastEvaluatedKey is returned (include it on the API call for getting the next chunk of cubes). hashtag update_cube-price POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_cube-price parameters JSON check method parameters hashtag update_cube-price Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id cubesList list A list of cubes whose price needs to be updated. Use cube data i and j indexes to identify each cube using the format i_j (e.g. 40_0). priceInCents number Price in USD cents (e.g. 500 to indicate a 5USD cube price) hashtag update_cube-price API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js hashtag update_cube-price Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the number of correctly updated cubes, the number of invalid detected cubes, and the list of invalid cubes. check method parameters
The maximum number of cubes returned (a field can have hundreds of cubes).
(optional) If more cubes are available, include the returned key to get the next chink of cubes.

pages.title: Event Types | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for defining Event Types in FAN Game Cube, representing in-game occurrences like goals, assists, or passes. Event Types are registered with point values and spatial coordinates, affecting cube owners whose zones match action locations.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new 'pass' event type worth 100 points for a specific field
Returns the unique event type ID for use in registering live events

useCases: Define goal events with high point values for cube owners
Create pass event types with moderate reward points
Set up assist events for secondary reward distribution
Configure foul or penalty events with negative points
Update event point values to balance gameplay rewards

implementationSteps.title: Create Event Type
implementationSteps.description: Use new_event-type operation with fieldId, eventTypeName (e.g., 'pass'), and eventTypePoints (e.g., '100')

implementationSteps.title: Store Event Type ID
implementationSteps.description: Save the returned eventTypeId to use when registering live events during matches

implementationSteps.title: Update or Delete Events
implementationSteps.description: Call update_event-type to change point values or delete_event-type to remove event types from the field

pages.title: Field | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API operations for managing Field objects, representing virtual sports venues tokenized into interactive zones. Fields serve as the foundational canvas for gameplay, typically linked to real-world stadiums, with dimensions defined in centimeters.

codeExamples.description: Creates a Camp Nou football field with 120x80 meter dimensions and 3x3 meter cubes
Returns the unique field ID after successful creation

useCases: Create Real Madrid Stadium field for Champions League matches
Set up Madison Square Garden for basketball gameplay
Tokenize Camp Nou into 3x3 meter NFT zones for fans
Define field dimensions in centimeters for precise cube placement
List all created fields or delete unused field configurations

implementationSteps.title: Define Field Dimensions
implementationSteps.description: Specify name, sport type, fieldLength and fieldWidth in centimeters, and cubeLength for NFT zone size (e.g., 300 for 3x3m)

implementationSteps.title: Create Field with API
implementationSteps.description: Call new_field operation with parameters to create the field and automatically generate all associated cubes

implementationSteps.title: Store Field ID
implementationSteps.description: Save the returned fieldId to link calendars, matches, and live events to this specific venue

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API chevron-right Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Cube API Field Represents a virtual sports field tokenized into interactive zones or sections ("CUBES"). It is the foundational canvas for all game interactions. Each field is typically linked to a real-world sports match. Examples: Real Madrid Stadium Madison Square Garden hashtag new_field POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_field parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Field name (e.g. Camp Nou) sport string Sport (e.g. Football) fieldLength number Field length in centimeters (e.g. 12000) fieldWidth number Field width in centimeters (e.g. 9000) cubeLength number The square size of a single cube (e.g. 300 for a cube of 3x3 meters) hashtag new_field API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js hashtag new_field Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the fieldId , which will be used to link NFT Game Cube related objects. All the cubes for that field are created when creating a new field. hashtag list_fields POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_fields parameters JSON (optional) check method parameters hashtag list_fields Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id hashtag list_fields API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js hashtag list_fields Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the list of fields you created or a single field if fieldId parameter is included in the call. hashtag delete_field POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_field parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag delete_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id to delete. hashtag delete_field API Call Example delete_field API call using Node.js hashtag delete_field Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. circle-info All the cubes created and assigned to the deleted field are also deleted. pages.title: Live Events | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API for recording live match events at specific coordinates on a Field during scheduled Matches. LiveEvent captures game data like goals, moves, or fouls, maps them to Cubes for scoring, and supports manual, AI-driven, or application-based event registration.

codeExamples.description: Registers a live event at coordinates (45, 40) during a match for cube reward processing

useCases: Record live match events with x,y coordinates for cube scoring
Integrate AI-powered goal detection for automatic event capture
Manually register chess moves via organizer dashboard
Process real-time fouls or plays for fan reward distribution
Map match events to cube owners for point accumulation

implementationSteps.title: Prepare Event Parameters
implementationSteps.description: Gather matchId, fieldId, calendarId, event coordinates (x, y), and eventTypeId for the specific event

implementationSteps.title: Register Live Event
implementationSteps.description: Call new_event operation with all parameters to record the event at specific field coordinates

implementationSteps.title: Process Cube Rewards
implementationSteps.description: System automatically identifies cube owners at event coordinates and applies points based on eventTypePoints

It is the primary mechanism by which game data—such as goals, moves, fouls, or plays—is captured and mapped to corresponding Cubes for scoring and reward processing. This API serves as a trusted interface to power fan engagement: Manually via the organizer dashboard or solution UI (BWS Solution Interface) Automatically via BWS-provided machine learning models that detect and push game events in real time (e.g., AI-driven goal recognition, chess move capture) Through your application. hashtag new_event POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_event parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_event Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id for which you want to register the event. fieldId string The Field Id the match is linked to. calendarId string The calendar Id the match is linked to. event JSON check event parameter hashtag new_event Event Parameter Parameter Type Desciption coordinates JSON check coordinates parameter eventTypeId string The Event Type Id of the event you want to register. hashtag new_event Coordinates Parameter Parameter Type Desciption x string The 2D x coordinate of the event. y string The 2D y coordinate of the event. hashtag new_event API Call Example new_event API call using Node.js hashtag new_event Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Match | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Match objects in Fan Game Cube. Matches are time-bound gameplay sessions tied to specific Fields where events are tracked, points awarded, and fan interactions occur. Includes operations for creating, updating, listing, and deleting matches with team information and status tracking.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new match in the NFT Game Cube system with team information, start time, and match image
Lists matches for a specific calendar, optionally filtering by match ID
Response when successfully creating a new match, returns the generated matchId

useCases: Create football match sessions like PSG vs Manchester City
Schedule basketball games for NBA teams like Green Bay Packers vs Chicago Bears
Organize chess tournament rounds between players
Track match status through scheduled, playing, finished, and canceled states
Set pricing for cube purchases per match in USD cents

implementationSteps.title: Create a new match
implementationSteps.description: Use new_match operation with calendarId, startTimeInMillis, team names, and optional match details including images (base64 encoded)

implementationSteps.title: Update match information
implementationSteps.description: Call update_match with matchId and only the parameters you want to change, such as status or description

implementationSteps.title: Monitor and manage matches
implementationSteps.description: Use list_matches to retrieve match details and delete_match to remove matches when needed

It represents a game instance—such as a football match, chess round, or tournament heat—scheduled via the Calendar object and hosted on a defined Field .\ Examples: PSG vs. Manchester City Green Bay Packers vs Chicago Bears Magnus Carlsen vs Hikaru Nakamura circle-info Images The images sent using base64 encoding are saved into IPFS using the BWS IPFS Solution. When getting object details, the IPFS URL is returned. hashtag new_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_match parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_match Method Parameters circle-info Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and startTimeInMillis). Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match should be included in. startTimeInMillis * number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled". priceInCents number The default price in USD cents for all the cubes if no specific price is defined for a cube (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD). hashtag new_match API Call Example new_match API call using Node.js hashtag new_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the newly created match. hashtag update_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_match parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag update_match Method Parameters circle-info Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and matchId) Only provide those you want to update. Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match is included in. matchId * string The Match Id we want to update. startTimeInMillis number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled" priceInCents number The price in USD cents (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD) hashtag update_match API Call Example update_match API call using Node.js hashtag update_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the updated match. hashtag list_matches POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_matches parameters JSON check method parameters hashtag list_matches Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption calendar_id string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. matchId string (optional) The Match Id we want to list. hashtag list_matches API Call Example list_matches API call using Node.js hashtag list_matches Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matches for the provided calendar. If we give a Match Id, it will just return the match we want to list. hashtag delete_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag delete_match Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id of the match we want to delete. calendarId string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. hashtag delete_match API Call Example delete_match API call using Node.js hashtag delete_match Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Plays (Field-Calendar) | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Field-Calendar relationships (Plays) in NFT Game Cube. This relationship models the temporal lifecycle of a Field by linking static field configurations with dynamic calendar entries representing individual matches, tournaments, or rounds. Enables organizing sports events across multiple seasons.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new field-calendar relationship to link a sports field with a season or tournament calendar
Lists all calendar entries linked to a specific field
Response showing field-calendar relationship with IPFS URL for uploaded images

useCases: Create PSG Champions League 2026 season on a football field
Organize New York Knicks 2026 NBA Season on a basketball court
Link multiple tournament calendars to a single chess board field
Manage different seasons for the same sports ground over time
Track field usage across various competitions and time periods

implementationSteps.title: Link field to calendar
implementationSteps.description: Use new_field-calendar operation with fieldId, calendarId, description (e.g., PSG 2025-26), and optional image in base64

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve calendar associations
implementationSteps.description: Call list_field-calendars with a fieldId to view all calendars linked to that field

implementationSteps.title: Remove outdated relationships
implementationSteps.description: Execute delete_field-calendar with both fieldId and calendarId to remove the relationship

pages.title: Prizes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Prize objects in NFT Game Cube. Prizes are reward assets that users receive based on CUBE performance in specific matches or seasons. Can include digital NFTs, token bonuses, physical goods, or experiences, configurable by solution owners to drive fan engagement.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new prize for a match with position (1st, 2nd, 3rd), name, description, and image
Lists all prizes configured for a specific match
Response when successfully creating a match prize, returns the generated prizeId

useCases: Award signed merchandise to top cube performers
Distribute VIP match tickets as prizes for first place
Offer Zoom calls or AMA sessions with athletes as rewards
Grant bonus BWS tokens to winning cube owners
Provide physical goods or experiences based on match performance

implementationSteps.title: Create match prizes
implementationSteps.description: Use new_match-prize with matchId, calendarId, position (1/2/3), name, description, and snapshot image in base64

implementationSteps.title: Update prize details
implementationSteps.description: Call update_match-prize with prizeId and modify fields like htmlDescription or additional images (image1, image2, image3)

implementationSteps.title: Manage prize catalog
implementationSteps.description: Use list_match-prizes to view all prizes for a match and delete_match-prize to remove prizes using prizeId

pages.title: FAN Game Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Overview of the FAN Game Cube platform for building new revenue streams in sports. Enables sports enthusiasts to own digital NFT assets linked to field sections across multiple sports including football, chess, tennis, cricket, basketball, rugby, and baseball. Features real-time engagement, prize mechanisms, and one-click purchase integration.
useCases: Tokenize football stadium sections into 500-2000 cubes for fan ownership
Enable chess tournament engagement by selling 64 chessboard squares as NFTs
Create tennis court cube divisions for baseline and service box ownership
Divide cricket fields into batting zones and fielding areas for fan participation
Generate revenue through basketball court key area tokenization including three-point arc and paint zone

implementationSteps.title: Define cube allocation strategy
implementationSteps.description: Determine how many cubes to create based on the sport (500-2000 for football, 64 for chess) and identify premium vs secondary zones for pricing

implementationSteps.title: Configure pricing model
implementationSteps.description: Set dynamic pricing based on field position value (prime areas at higher prices, secondary zones at $10 or lower), calculate expected revenue as cubes × average price

implementationSteps.title: Deploy purchase widget
implementationSteps.description: Integrate the one-click JavaScript purchase widget into your website for seamless cube purchases with credit cards

implementationSteps.title: Enable fan engagement
implementationSteps.description: Provide mobile app access for fans to manage cube collections, track live matches, and join leaderboards for prize competitions

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview Building New Revenue Streams for the Sports Industry BWS's FAN Game Cube platform revolutionizes fan engagement by allowing sports enthusiasts to own digital assets linked to specific sections of a sports field. This extends beyond football to include chess, tennis, cricket, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, and any sport with a field that can be split into "cubes". As real-time events unfold in their selected area, fans earn points and unlock exciting rewards, all while creating new revenue opportunities for sports clubs, former athletes, influencers, and other key figures in the sports industry. Want to launch a FAN Game Cube event? Contact us here arrow-up-right . hashtag Cube Allocation for Different Sports Sports organizers can define how many cubes to sell and how to divide their playing area based on the sport’s unique characteristics. A football/soccer field cubes division. Examples include: Football stadiums It is typically divided into 500, 1000, or 2000 cubes , ensuring key areas like penalty boxes and midfield zones hold premium value. Chess tournaments The 64 chessboard squares can be tokenized, offering fans a unique way to interact with their favorite chess events. Tennis courts Tokenizing the baseline, service boxes, and net area allows fans to engage in real-time match events. Cricket fields Dividing the pitch into batting zones, fielding areas, and boundary sections enhances fan participation. Basketball courts Cube allocation can include key areas like the three-point arc, free-throw line, and paint zone . Rugby fields Fans can purchase cubes covering try zones, scrums, and midfield sections . Baseball fields Key locations such as bases, outfield zones, and pitcher’s mound can be tokenized. And a long etcetera. hashtag NFT Technology for Ownership and Trading FAN Game Cube utilizes NFT technology to ensure verifiable ownership. However, the usage of NFT goes beyond ownership, allowing fans to trade their cubes as they would with traditional assets. During a season or tournament, cube owners can buy and sell cubes mid-season on secondary marketplaces, generating additional royalties for sports clubs and organizers. Since NFTs offer permanent and transparent ownership records , fans gain long-term value while clubs benefit from secondary sales. hashtag Pricing and Monetization Strategy Organizers have complete control over pricing, ensuring dynamic valuation based on strategic cube placement. Different pricing strategies include: Prime field areas Higher prices can be charged for high-demand sections, such as the center of a football pitch, penalty spots, or key cricket fielding positions. Secondary zones To encourage broad participation, less strategic locations, such as side areas or backfield positions, can be offered at $10 or lower. Dynamic pricing models Pricing can fluctuate based on match importance, demand, and historical engagement levels. hashtag Revenue Potential Your expected revenue is calculated as follows: Number of cubes × Average Price per cube = Total income For example, launching 500 cubes at an average price of $500 per cube on a VIP event can generate $250,000 in revenue. Larger stadiums, premium tournaments, or full-season FAN Game Cubes can scale this model to generate even higher revenues. hashtag Seamless Integration with One-Click Purchase Widget BWS provides a one-click purchase widget for frictionless transactions. Sports clubs do not need to create special accounts or navigate complex payment gateways—cubes can be sold in just a few hours. A weekend Chess Tournament NFT Game Cube event. The widget integrates easily into existing club websites, allowing fans to purchase cubes as simply as buying a regular game ticket. hashtag How Fans Can Participate and Win Once a sports club publishes its one-click buy widget , fans can easily purchase FAN Game Cubes using standard credit cards, making participation straightforward and accessible. One-click FAN Game Cube purchase experience This experience brings a new layer of engagement to the fan experience while giving them direct ownership of in-game digital assets. hashtag Real-Time Fan Engagement and Play & Win Mechanics NFT-based cubes offer fans an engaging way to experience their favorite sports, providing: True ownership of digital sports assets. Fans can buy, sell, or hold tradeable assets for future value (full-season FAN Game Cube). Passive earnings potential through resale royalties and long-term value accumulation. Access to real-time engagement through a dedicated mobile app. Once a cube is purchased, fans can: Manage their cube collection across multiple sports. Track upcoming matches in real time. Join live leaderboards to compete with other fans for top prizes. hashtag Why Choose FAN Game Cube? Multi-Sport Scalability Works across various sports, including football, cricket, chess, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, golf, Formula 1, and esports. It is flexible and adaptable to different event structures and fan bases. One-Click Buy Widget A JavaScript widget for effortless integration that enhances the club’s purchasing experience with quick, seamless transactions. Easy & Free to Launch Clubs and organizers can set up Fan Game Cube events using a simple three-step workflow or API calls. Flexible Event Calendars Supports single VIP games, full-season matches, and tournaments. It is scalable, from small local events like the Ohio Chess Tournament to major leagues like NBA teams. Real-Time Fan Engagement A comprehensive mobile app enables clubs to foster deep connections with fans during matches. Features include live-streaming for real-time game viewing and interactive chat to enhance fan engagement and discussion. BWS Fan Game Cube is the future of sports engagement, bridging the digital and real-world experiences for fans while unlocking new revenue opportunities for sports organizations. Get started today and transform the way fans interact with their favorite games! pages.title: X Bot | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Overview of X Bot, a Telegram-based analytics platform providing accurate X (Twitter) engagement metrics using the official X API. Documents 73 commands across 12 categories including filtering, tracking, configuration, raids, analytics, and administration for community engagement monitoring.
useCases: Track X (Twitter) account engagement metrics for community members
Coordinate engagement raids to boost post visibility and reach
Generate automated performance reports on scheduled intervals
Monitor keyword and cashtag mentions across Twitter
Create custom leaderboards with configurable scoring for likes, retweets, and replies

implementationSteps.title: Initialize bot
implementationSteps.description: Run /start command to set up the bot in your Telegram group

implementationSteps.title: Add tracking filters
implementationSteps.description: Use /add_accounts to track X accounts, /add_keywords for keyword monitoring, and /add_cashtags for token symbols

implementationSteps.title: Generate first report
implementationSteps.description: Execute /recreate command to fetch latest X data and create analytics leaderboard

pages.title: X Bot Commands | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Complete command reference for all 73 X Bot commands organized by 12 functional categories. Covers filter setup, basic operations, advanced filtering, project configuration, raid commands, scheduling, reports, leaderboard customization, topic management, admin functions, and subscription management.
useCases: Set up first-time bot configuration with account tracking and project naming
Execute daily operations including report viewing and raid coordination
Configure advanced filtering with named filters and complex tracking scenarios
Automate report generation with scheduled intervals
Customize engagement scoring and leaderboard display preferences

implementationSteps.title: Initial setup
implementationSteps.description: Run /start to initialize, /add_accounts to track X accounts, /set_project_name for configuration, and /recreate to generate first report

implementationSteps.title: Daily operations workflow
implementationSteps.description: Use /report to view latest analytics, /raidx to start engagement raids, and /raid_status to monitor raid progress

implementationSteps.title: Advanced configuration
implementationSteps.description: Apply /set_x_filtering for complex tracking, /set_schedule for automation, and /set_points to customize engagement scoring

Initialize the bot for your group /add_accounts arrow-up-right - Add X accounts to track /set_project_name arrow-up-right - Configure project name /recreate arrow-up-right - Generate your first analytics report hashtag Daily Operations /report arrow-up-right - View latest performance analytics /raidx arrow-up-right - Start a new engagement raid /raid_status arrow-up-right - Check active raid progress hashtag Advanced Configuration /set_x_filtering arrow-up-right - Create named filters for complex tracking /set_schedule arrow-up-right - Automate report generation /set_points arrow-up-right - Customize engagement scoring hashtag All Commands by Category hashtag Filter Setup (20 commands) Manage tracking filters for accounts, keywords, cashtags, mentions, exclusions, and ignore lists. Commands: /list_filters arrow-up-right - Lists all configured filters for the current group, showing their names and a summary of what each. /show_filter arrow-up-right - Displays the complete configuration of a filter including all tracked accounts, keywords, cashtags,. /add_accounts arrow-up-right - Adds one or more X (Twitter) accounts to track. /add_keywords arrow-up-right - Adds keywords or phrases to track in X posts. /add_cashtags arrow-up-right - Adds cashtags (token symbols) to track in X posts. /add_mentions arrow-up-right - Adds X handles to track when they are mentioned in posts. /add_excludes arrow-up-right - Adds keywords or phrases to exclude from tracking. /add_ignore arrow-up-right - Adds X accounts to ignore. /remove_accounts arrow-up-right - Removes specific X accounts from tracking. /remove_keywords arrow-up-right - Removes specific keywords or phrases from tracking. /remove_cashtags arrow-up-right - Removes specific cashtags from tracking. /remove_mentions arrow-up-right - Removes X handles from mention tracking. /remove_excludes arrow-up-right - Removes keywords from the exclusion list. /remove_ignore arrow-up-right - Removes X accounts from the ignore list. /clear_accounts arrow-up-right - Removes ALL tracked accounts from the filter. /clear_keywords arrow-up-right - Removes ALL keywords from the filter. /clear_cashtags arrow-up-right - Removes ALL cashtags from the filter. /clear_mentions arrow-up-right - Removes ALL mentions from the filter. /clear_excludes arrow-up-right - Removes ALL excluded keywords from the filter. /clear_ignore arrow-up-right - Removes ALL ignored accounts from the filter. hashtag Basic Commands (4 commands) Essential bot operations including help, initialization, and status checks. Commands: /help arrow-up-right - Displays a complete list of available bot commands and their descriptions. /start arrow-up-right - Initializes the bot and provides welcome messages. /status arrow-up-right - Checks if the bot is operational and running properly in your group. /get_chatid arrow-up-right - Displays your Telegram group's unique chat ID. hashtag Advanced Filtering (3 commands) Create named filters for complex tracking scenarios and multi-project management. Commands: /set_x_filtering arrow-up-right - Creates advanced named filters with complex X API query syntax. /get_x_filtering arrow-up-right - Lists all advanced named filters configured for your group. /delete_x_filtering arrow-up-right - Deletes advanced named filters created with /set_x_filtering. hashtag Project Configuration (10 commands) Customize project metadata including name, description, logo, and URLs for reports. Commands: /set_project_name arrow-up-right - Sets your project or community name that will appear in all generated reports, leaderboards, and on the. /get_project_name arrow-up-right - Displays the current project name configured for your community. /set_project_description arrow-up-right - Sets a short description of your project that appears in reports and on the website. /get_project_description arrow-up-right - Displays the current project description configured for your community. /set_project_long_description arrow-up-right - Sets a detailed, comprehensive description of your project for the website dashboard. /get_project_long_description arrow-up-right - Displays the current detailed project description configured for the website dashboard. /set_project_logo arrow-up-right - Uploads your project logo that will appear on reports and the website dashboard. /get_project_logo arrow-up-right - Displays the URL of your currently configured project logo. /set_project_urls arrow-up-right - Sets important links for your project including website, social media, documentation, and other resources. /get_project_urls arrow-up-right - Displays all project URLs currently configured for your community. hashtag Raid Commands (11 commands) Coordinate X (Twitter) engagement raids to amplify post visibility and reach. Commands: /raidx arrow-up-right - Launches a coordinated community raid on an X (Twitter) post to boost engagement. /raid_status arrow-up-right - Displays real-time progress of active raids, showing current engagement numbers vs. /raid_stop arrow-up-right - Cancels an active raid immediately, stopping progress tracking and notifications. /raid_history arrow-up-right - Displays a list of previous raids with their completion status, targets, and results. /set_raid_defaults arrow-up-right - Configures default settings for all future raids including target engagement numbers, duration, auto-mute behavior,. /get_raid_defaults arrow-up-right - Displays the current default raid settings including target numbers for all engagement types, default duration,. /set_raid_message_behavior arrow-up-right - Controls how the bot updates raid progress messages in the chat. /get_raid_message_behavior arrow-up-right - Shows the currently configured raid message update mode (edit, repost, or pin) and explains what that mode does. /set_raid_image arrow-up-right - Uploads a custom image that appears in all raid announcement messages for your community. /auto_raid_config arrow-up-right - Configures automatic raids that trigger for the "Best Tweet of the Day" selected during daily report generation. /get_auto_raid_config arrow-up-right - Displays the current auto-raid configuration including whether it's enabled, the raid duration, start delay after. hashtag Schedule Management (3 commands) Configure automated report generation schedules with custom intervals. Commands: /set_schedule arrow-up-right - Sets a schedule for when reports should be automatically generated. /get_schedule arrow-up-right - Retrieves and displays the current scheduled time for automatic report generation. /delete_schedule arrow-up-right - Removes the configured daily schedule for automatic report generation. hashtag Calendar & Time (2 commands) Manage calendar settings and time periods for report generation cycles. Commands: /set_calendar arrow-up-right - Sets up a recurring calendar schedule for automatic report generation. /get_calendar arrow-up-right - Displays the current calendar schedule configuration for automatic reports, showing the start date and how often. hashtag Reports (2 commands) Generate and manage performance analytics reports for tracked X content. Commands: /report arrow-up-right - Displays the most recently generated leaderboard report for your community. /recreate arrow-up-right - Triggers immediate generation of a fresh report by fetching latest X (Twitter) data and creating new leaderboards. hashtag Leaderboard Customization (10 commands) Customize scoring points, colors, titles, and display settings for leaderboard reports. Commands: /set_points arrow-up-right - Sets the scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views). /get_points arrow-up-right - Retrieves and displays the current scoring multipliers for engagement metrics. /set_colors arrow-up-right - Sets the HTML colors for displaying engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views). /get_colors arrow-up-right - Retrieves and displays the current HTML color configuration for engagement metrics. /set_top_title arrow-up-right - Sets the title text and color for the main leaderboard section in generated reports. /set_best_title arrow-up-right - Sets the title text and color for the best tweet section in generated reports. /set_engagement_title arrow-up-right - Sets the title text and color for the engagement scoring section in generated reports. /get_titles arrow-up-right - Retrieves and displays all current title configurations for the report sections including. /set_top_count arrow-up-right - Sets the number of users to display in the report leaderboard. /get_top_count arrow-up-right - Retrieves and displays the current number of users configured to show in the report. hashtag Topic Management (2 commands) Configure Telegram topic/thread settings for bot operation. Commands: /set_topic arrow-up-right - Configures which forum topic (thread) the bot should send reports to in Telegram groups that use forum-style topics. /get_topic arrow-up-right - Displays which forum topic (thread) is currently configured to receive bot reports. hashtag Admin (1 commands) Administrative functions for managing bot notifications and permissions. Commands: /add_admin arrow-up-right - Designates specific group administrators to receive important bot notifications via private messages, such as. hashtag Subscription & Credits (5 commands) Manage billing, subscriptions, credits, and usage tracking. Commands: /subscription arrow-up-right - Provides access to Stripe customer portal for subscription management including billing history,. /buy arrow-up-right - Initiates the purchase process for X Bot Professional subscription. /get_credits arrow-up-right - Displays the current credit usage and availability for the bot. /payment_success arrow-up-right - Handles successful payment confirmation after Stripe checkout completion. /payment_cancelled arrow-up-right - Handles payment cancellation when users exit Stripe checkout without completing payment. pages.title: Admin | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Admin command reference for designating specific group administrators to receive bot notifications. Allows limiting alerts like credit exhaustion warnings, subscription renewals, and error messages to selected admins instead of all group administrators, reducing notification noise.

codeExamples.description: Designates multiple administrators to receive bot notifications and alerts
Clears designated admin list and reverts to notifying all group administrators

useCases: Limit credit exhaustion warnings to billing administrators only
Route subscription renewal notifications to designated bot managers
Send error alerts to technical admins responsible for bot maintenance
Reduce notification noise for group admins not involved in bot management
Control which administrators receive important bot status updates

By default, the bot sends notifications to all group admins, but you can use this command to limit notifications to specific admins who manage the bot. This helps reduce notification noise for admins who don't need bot alerts. All designated users must already be group administrators. paragraphs: Designates specific group administrators to receive important bot notifications via private messages, such as credit exhaustion warnings, subscription renewals, or error alerts. By default, the bot sends notifications to all group admins, but you can use this command to limit notifications to specific admins who manage the bot. This helps reduce notification noise for admins who don't need bot alerts. All designated users must already be group administrators.

pages.title: Advanced Filtering | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Advanced filtering commands for creating named filters with complex X API query syntax. Supports Boolean logic with OR/AND operators and special operators including from:, keywords:, mention:, exclude:, and ignore: for sophisticated multi-condition tracking scenarios beyond simple add/remove commands.

codeExamples.description: Creates a named filter to track mentions of a project handle or hashtag
Creates a filter tracking tweets from specific KOL accounts containing the BWS keyword
Creates a filter for trending hashtags while excluding spam content and ignoring specific accounts

useCases: Track multiple KOL accounts discussing specific keywords with single filter
Monitor project mentions across handles and hashtags simultaneously
Create trending topic filters with spam and scam exclusions
Combine account tracking with keyword requirements using Boolean logic
Build complex multi-condition filters for sophisticated tracking scenarios

implementationSteps.title: Create named filter
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_x_filtering with a filter name and X API query syntax combining operators like from:, keywords:, mention:, exclude:, and Boolean logic

implementationSteps.title: Review configured filters
implementationSteps.description: Execute /get_x_filtering to list all advanced filters with their names and query syntax

implementationSteps.title: Delete filters
implementationSteps.description: Remove specific filters by name or use wildcard '*' with /delete_x_filtering to clear all advanced filters

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Advanced Filtering hashtag /set_x_filtering Creates advanced named filters with complex X API query syntax. Supports combining multiple criteria using logical operators (OR, AND) and special operators (from:, keywords:, mention:, exclude:, ignore:). Use this for sophisticated tracking scenarios requiring multiple conditions and Boolean logic that go beyond simple add/remove commands. Admin Only Copy /set_x_filtering project_mentions mention:@MyProject OR #MyProjectToken Copy /set_x_filtering my_kols from:@CryptoHayes OR from:@IncomeSharks keywords:BWS Copy /set_x_filtering trending #DeFi OR #crypto OR #blockchain exclude:scam ignore:@SpamBot Parameters: Name Type Example filter_name required my_filter query_syntax required value hashtag /get_x_filtering Lists all advanced named filters configured for your group. Shows each filter's name and its complete X API query syntax, helping you understand what content is being tracked. Use this to review your sophisticated filtering setup created with /set_x_filtering. Admin Only Copy /get_x_filtering hashtag /delete_x_filtering Deletes advanced named filters created with /set_x_filtering. Remove a specific filter by name to stop tracking that particular query, or use the wildcard "*" to delete all advanced filters at once. This does not affect simple filters managed with add/remove commands. paragraphs: Creates advanced named filters with complex X API query syntax. Supports combining multiple criteria using logical operators (OR, AND) and special operators (from:, keywords:, mention:, exclude:, ignore:). Use this for sophisticated tracking scenarios requiring multiple conditions and Boolean logic that go beyond simple add/remove commands.

Lists all advanced named filters configured for your group. Shows each filter's name and its complete X API query syntax, helping you understand what content is being tracked. Use this to review your sophisticated filtering setup created with /set_x_filtering.

Deletes advanced named filters created with /set_x_filtering. Remove a specific filter by name to stop tracking that particular query, or use the wildcard "*" to delete all advanced filters at once. This does not affect simple filters managed with add/remove commands.

pages.title: Basic Commands | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Basic bot command reference covering essential operations including help display, initialization, status checks, and chat ID retrieval. Commands show different functionality for administrators versus regular users, with setup guidance and operational verification capabilities.

codeExamples.description: Displays complete list of available commands - full list for admins, limited list for regular users
Initializes the bot in group chats or shows welcome message with 'Add to Group' button in private messages
Checks if bot is operational and provides documentation link for troubleshooting
Displays Telegram group's unique chat ID for technical configurations and API integrations

useCases: Discover available bot commands and their descriptions with /help
Initialize bot in new Telegram groups for first-time setup
Verify bot responsiveness after installation or during troubleshooting
Retrieve chat ID for advanced configurations and system integrations
Handle payment confirmations after subscription purchases

Group administrators see all commands including advanced configuration options, while regular users see a limited list of basic commands they can use. Use this to discover what the bot can do and get quick command references. Admin Only Copy /help hashtag /start Initializes the bot and provides welcome messages. In group chats, confirms the bot is running and directs you to use /help for available commands. In private messages with the bot, shows a welcome message with an "Add to Group" button to install the bot in your Telegram group. Also handles payment confirmations when you complete a subscription purchase. Admin Only Copy /start hashtag /status Checks if the bot is operational and running properly in your group. Confirms the bot is active and provides a link to the documentation for setup instructions. Useful for verifying the bot is responsive after installation or troubleshooting connection issues. Admin Only Copy /status hashtag /get_chatid Displays your Telegram group's unique chat ID. This identifier is useful for technical configurations, integrations with other systems, or when contacting support. The chat ID is a unique number that Telegram assigns to each group and is required for certain advanced features and API integrations. pages.title: Calendar & Time | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Calendar and time management commands for setting up recurring report schedules. Enables automatic report generation at specified intervals with configurable start dates and cadence periods. All times operate in UTC timezone with support for past dates up to 6 days.

codeExamples.description: Sets up weekly automatic report generation starting June 20, 2025
Displays current calendar schedule configuration showing start date and report generation cadence

useCases: Schedule weekly reports every 7 days starting from a specific date
Configure automated report generation for regular community updates
Set up recurring analytics reports at custom day intervals
Verify current report generation schedule settings
Adjust report timing based on community activity patterns

implementationSteps.title: Set calendar schedule
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_calendar with start date (DD/MM/YYYY format) and cadence (e.g., 7days) to configure recurring reports

implementationSteps.title: Verify schedule
implementationSteps.description: Execute /get_calendar to confirm the configured start date and report generation interval

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Calendar & Time hashtag /set_calendar Sets up a recurring calendar schedule for automatic report generation. Specify a start date and how often reports should be generated (cadence in days). The bot will automatically create reports at the specified intervals starting from the chosen date. For example, setting "20/06/2025 7days" will generate weekly reports starting June 20, 2025. The start date can be up to 6 days in the past or any future date. All times are in UTC timezone. Admin Only Copy /set_calendar 20/06/2025 7days Parameters: Name Type Example start_date required 20/06/2025 cadence required 7days hashtag /get_calendar Displays the current calendar schedule configuration for automatic reports, showing the start date and how often reports are generated (cadence). Use this to verify your report generation schedule or to check if a calendar has been configured yet. All times are shown in UTC timezone. The start date can be up to 6 days in the past or any future date. All times are in UTC timezone.

pages.title: Filter Setup | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Comprehensive reference documentation for X Bot filter setup commands. Covers viewing, adding, removing, and clearing filters for tracking X (Twitter) accounts, keywords, cashtags, mentions, exclusions, and ignored accounts. All commands are admin-only and support optional named filters for organizing different tracking scenarios.

codeExamples.description: Add X (Twitter) accounts to track their posts in reports and leaderboards
Add keywords and hashtags to track in X posts, using quotes for multi-word phrases
Add cashtags to a named filter group for organized token symbol tracking

useCases: Track competitor X accounts and analyze their engagement strategies
Monitor brand mentions and hashtags across Twitter conversations
Filter out spam accounts and irrelevant content from community reports
Organize tracking into named filters like team, influencers, and competitors
Track cryptocurrency cashtags for token-specific sentiment analysis

implementationSteps.title: View Current Filters
implementationSteps.description: Use /list_filters to see all configured filters and /show_filter to view detailed configuration including tracked accounts, keywords, and exclusions

implementationSteps.title: Add Tracking Criteria
implementationSteps.description: Use /add_accounts, /add_keywords, /add_cashtags, and /add_mentions commands to define what content to track, optionally organizing with named filters

implementationSteps.title: Exclude Unwanted Content
implementationSteps.description: Use /add_excludes for spam keywords and /add_ignore for bot accounts to filter out noise from reports

implementationSteps.title: Manage and Refine
implementationSteps.description: Use /remove_* commands to delete specific items or /clear_* commands to reset entire filter categories when reorganizing tracking setup

Admin Only Copy /show_filter Copy /show_filter filter=kols Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional kols hashtag Add Filters hashtag /add_accounts Adds one or more X (Twitter) accounts to track. Posts from these accounts will be monitored and included in reports and leaderboards. You can optionally specify a named filter to organize different account groups (e.g., team members, influencers, competitors). Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example accounts required @CryptoHayes filter optional team hashtag /add_keywords Adds keywords or phrases to track in X posts. Any post containing these keywords will be monitored and included in reports. Use quotes for multi-word phrases. Keywords can include hashtags. Helps filter content by topics, brands, or specific terms relevant to your project. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example keywords required crypto filter optional trending hashtag /add_cashtags Adds cashtags (token symbols) to track in X posts. Any post containing these cashtags will be monitored and included in reports. Cashtags must start with $ symbol. Useful for tracking cryptocurrency tokens, stock symbols, or project-specific ticker symbols. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example cashtags required $BWS filter optional tokens hashtag /add_mentions Adds X handles to track when they are mentioned in posts. Any post that mentions these accounts (using @handle) will be monitored and included in reports. Different from add_accounts which tracks posts FROM accounts - this tracks posts ABOUT/MENTIONING accounts. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example mentions required value filter optional influencers hashtag /add_excludes Adds keywords or phrases to exclude from tracking. Posts containing these excluded terms will be filtered out and NOT included in reports, even if they match other filter criteria. Use this to remove noise, spam, or irrelevant content from your tracking. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example keywords required spam filter optional clean hashtag /add_ignore Adds X accounts to ignore. Posts from these accounts will be filtered out and NOT included in reports, even if they match other filter criteria. Use this to exclude bot accounts, spam accounts, or specific users whose posts you don't want to track. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example accounts required @SpamBot filter optional clean hashtag Remove/Clear Filters hashtag /remove_accounts Removes specific X accounts from tracking. Posts from these accounts will no longer be monitored or included in reports. Use this to stop tracking accounts that are no longer relevant to your project. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example accounts required @OldAccount filter optional kols hashtag /remove_keywords Removes specific keywords or phrases from tracking. Posts containing only these keywords will no longer be monitored. Use quotes for multi-word phrases. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example keywords required oldterm filter optional trending hashtag /remove_cashtags Removes specific cashtags from tracking. Posts containing only these cashtags will no longer be monitored. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example cashtags required $OLDTOKEN filter optional tokens hashtag /remove_mentions Removes X handles from mention tracking. Posts mentioning only these accounts will no longer be monitored. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example mentions required value filter optional influencers hashtag /remove_excludes Removes keywords from the exclusion list. Posts containing these terms will no longer be automatically filtered out. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example keywords required spam filter optional clean hashtag /remove_ignore Removes X accounts from the ignore list. Posts from these accounts will now be tracked if they match filter criteria. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example accounts required @ReinstatedAccount filter optional clean hashtag /clear_accounts Removes ALL tracked accounts from the filter. Use this to reset account tracking and start fresh. This does not delete the filter itself, only clears the accounts list. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional kols hashtag /clear_keywords Removes ALL keywords from the filter. Use this to reset keyword tracking. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional trending hashtag /clear_cashtags Removes ALL cashtags from the filter. Use this to reset cashtag tracking. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional tokens hashtag /clear_mentions Removes ALL mentions from the filter. Use this to reset mention tracking. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional influencers hashtag /clear_excludes Removes ALL excluded keywords from the filter. Posts with previously excluded terms will now be tracked. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional clean hashtag /clear_ignore Removes ALL ignored accounts from the filter. Posts from previously ignored accounts will now be tracked.
