pages.title: Leaderboard Customization | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation for customizing X Bot leaderboard appearance and scoring. Covers commands for setting point multipliers for engagement metrics, configuring HTML colors for visual reports, customizing section titles, and controlling the number of users displayed. All settings are admin-only and affect how leaderboard reports are generated and displayed.

codeExamples.description: Configure scoring multipliers for likes, retweets, replies, quotes, and views to calculate leaderboard rankings
Set HTML colors for displaying engagement metrics in leaderboard reports
Customize the title text and color for the main leaderboard section in reports

useCases: Weight replies higher than likes to prioritize conversation engagement
Customize report colors to match project branding and visual identity
Set custom leaderboard titles for weekly, monthly, or campaign-specific reports
Display top 20 performers instead of default 10 for larger communities
Create engagement scoring that aligns with project-specific KPIs

implementationSteps.title: Configure Point Multipliers
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_points to define how likes, retweets, replies, quotes, and views contribute to engagement scores, prioritizing metrics that matter most to your community

implementationSteps.title: Customize Visual Appearance
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_colors to set brand-aligned colors and /set_top_title, /set_best_title, /set_engagement_title to customize section headings

implementationSteps.title: Adjust Display Settings
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_top_count to control how many top performers appear in reports, and verify all settings with /get_points, /get_colors, and /get_titles commands

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Leaderboard Customization hashtag /set_points Sets the scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard calculations. These multipliers determine how many points each type of engagement contributes to a user's total score. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Copy /set_points 5 1 20 10 0.01 Parameters: Name Type Example likes required 50 retweets required 50 replies required 50 quotes required 50 views required value hashtag /get_points Retrieves and displays the current scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard calculations. Shows either configured values or indicates default values are being used. Copy /get_points hashtag /set_colors Sets the HTML colors for displaying engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) in the leaderboard reports. Colors are stored as hexadecimal values and used for report generation. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example likes required 50 retweets required 50 replies required 50 quotes required 50 views required value hashtag /get_colors Retrieves and displays the current HTML color configuration for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard reports. Shows either configured colors or default values if none are set. hashtag /set_top_title Sets the title text and color for the main leaderboard section in generated reports. Allows customization of the primary heading displayed at the top of leaderboard reports. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example title_text required My Title color required #FF5733 hashtag /set_best_title Sets the title text and color for the best tweet section in generated reports. Allows customization of the heading displayed above the highlighted best tweet. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example title_text required My Title color required #FF5733 hashtag /set_engagement_title Sets the title text and color for the engagement scoring section in generated reports. Allows customization of the heading displayed above engagement scoring rules and metrics. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example title_text required My Title color required #FF5733 hashtag /get_titles Retrieves and displays all current title configurations for the report sections including top title (main leaderboard), best title (best tweet), and engagement title (scoring rules). Shows either configured values or default values for each section. hashtag /set_top_count Sets the number of users to display in the report leaderboard. This controls how many top-performing users are shown in the generated reports. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example count required value hashtag /get_top_count Retrieves and displays the current number of users configured to show in the report leaderboard. Shows either the configured value or indicates default value (10) is being used. paragraphs: Sets the scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard calculations. These multipliers determine how many points each type of engagement contributes to a user's total score. Only group administrators can execute this command.

Retrieves and displays the current scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard calculations. Shows either configured values or indicates default values are being used.

Sets the HTML colors for displaying engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) in the leaderboard reports. Colors are stored as hexadecimal values and used for report generation. Only group administrators can execute this command.

Retrieves and displays the current HTML color configuration for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard reports. Shows either configured colors or default values if none are set.

Sets the title text and color for the main leaderboard section in generated reports. Allows customization of the primary heading displayed at the top of leaderboard reports. Only group administrators can execute this command.

Sets the title text and color for the best tweet section in generated reports. Allows customization of the heading displayed above the highlighted best tweet. Only group administrators can execute this command.

Sets the title text and color for the engagement scoring section in generated reports. Allows customization of the heading displayed above engagement scoring rules and metrics. Only group administrators can execute this command.

Retrieves and displays all current title configurations for the report sections including top title (main leaderboard), best title (best tweet), and engagement title (scoring rules). Shows either configured values or default values for each section.

pages.title: Project Configuration | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Reference documentation for configuring project identity and branding in X Bot. Covers commands for setting project name, descriptions (short and long), logo upload, and project URLs. These settings customize how the project appears in generated reports and on the public xbot.ninja dashboard website.

codeExamples.description: Set the project name that appears in all reports and on the website dashboard

codeExamples.code: /set_project_description A revolutionary DeFi platform for community governance
codeExamples.description: Set a concise 1-2 sentence project description for reports and website
Configure project website and social media links for the dashboard

useCases: Customize reports with official project branding before sharing with investors
Create a public project dashboard on xbot.ninja for investor discovery
Upload project logo to maintain consistent brand identity across all reports
Add comprehensive project description and links for transparency
Replace generic Telegram group names with professional project identity

implementationSteps.title: Set Basic Project Information
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_project_name to establish project identity and /set_project_description for a concise summary that appears in reports

implementationSteps.title: Add Detailed Information
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_project_long_description for comprehensive website dashboard content covering mission, features, roadmap, and team details

implementationSteps.title: Upload Branding Assets
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_project_logo to upload your logo image and /set_project_urls to add website, social media, and documentation links for complete project presentation

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Project Configuration hashtag /set_project_name Sets your project or community name that will appear in all generated reports, leaderboards, and on the website dashboard. This is the main identifier for your community and should be your project's official name or brand. Use this to customize reports with your project's identity instead of using generic Telegram group names. Admin Only Copy /set_project_name CryptoDAO Community Copy /set_project_name My Awesome Project Parameters: Name Type Example project_name required My Project hashtag /get_project_name Displays the current project name configured for your community. Use this to verify what name will appear in reports and on the website, or to check if a project name has been set yet. Admin Only Copy /get_project_name hashtag /set_project_description Sets a short description of your project that appears in reports and on the website. This should be a concise summary of what your project does or your community's mission (1-2 sentences). Use this to provide context about your project's goals when sharing reports with investors, partners, or community members. Admin Only Copy /set_project_description A revolutionary DeFi platform for community governance Parameters: Name Type Example description required value hashtag /get_project_description Displays the current project description configured for your community. Use this to review the short description that appears in reports, or to check if a description has been set. Admin Only hashtag /set_project_long_description Sets a detailed, comprehensive description of your project for the website dashboard. Unlike the short description, this can be multiple paragraphs and include more in-depth information about your project's mission, features, roadmap, and team. This appears on your public dashboard page to provide visitors with complete project details. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example long_description required value hashtag /get_project_long_description Displays the current detailed project description configured for the website dashboard. Use this to review the comprehensive description that visitors see on your public dashboard page, or to check if a long description has been set. Admin Only hashtag /set_project_logo Uploads your project logo that will appear on reports and the website dashboard. After running this command, the bot will prompt you to send an image file. Send your logo as a photo or file (PNG, JPG, or GIF recommended). The logo should be square or rectangular and will be displayed prominently in all generated reports and on your public dashboard page. Admin Only hashtag /get_project_logo Displays the URL of your currently configured project logo. Use this to verify which logo will appear in reports and on the website, or to share the logo URL with team members. If no logo is set, you'll receive a message prompting you to upload one with /set_project_logo. Admin Only hashtag /set_project_urls Sets important links for your project including website, social media, documentation, and other resources. These URLs appear on the website dashboard as clickable links, making it easy for visitors to connect with your project across different platforms. You can include your website, Twitter/X, Discord, Telegram, GitHub, documentation, or any other relevant links. Separate multiple URLs with spaces or commas. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example urls required https://myproject.com hashtag /get_project_urls Displays all project URLs currently configured for your community. Use this to review which links appear on the website dashboard, verify URLs are correct, or check if any URLs have been set. pages.title: Raid Commands | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation for X Bot raid commands that enable coordinated community engagement campaigns on X (Twitter) posts. Covers launching raids with customizable targets, tracking real-time progress, managing active raids, reviewing raid history, and configuring default settings. Includes auto-raid functionality for daily best tweets.

codeExamples.description: Launch a raid on a specific X post with target engagement goals for likes and retweets
Display real-time progress of the latest active raid showing current vs. target engagement metrics
Configure default raid settings including target engagement numbers and duration

useCases: Amplify important project announcements by coordinating community engagement
Support partnership posts with organized community retweets and likes
Drive visibility for product launches through coordinated X campaigns
Automatically raid the best daily tweet to reward top community content
Track campaign effectiveness with real-time progress monitoring

implementationSteps.title: Configure Raid Defaults
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_raid_defaults to set standard target numbers for likes, retweets, replies, quotes, and bookmarks, plus default duration and mute behavior

implementationSteps.title: Launch a Raid
implementationSteps.description: Use /raidx with the target X post URL and optional custom parameters to start a coordinated engagement campaign, overriding defaults as needed

implementationSteps.title: Monitor and Manage
implementationSteps.description: Use /raid_status to track real-time progress, /raid_stop to cancel if needed, and /raid_history to review past campaigns and analyze performance patterns

implementationSteps.title: Enable Auto-Raids
implementationSteps.description: Use /auto_raid_config to automatically raid the best tweet from daily reports, sustaining engagement on top community content without manual intervention

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Raid Commands hashtag /raidx Launches a coordinated community raid on an X (Twitter) post to boost engagement. Community members receive a raid announcement with the target post and engagement goals (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks). The bot tracks progress in real-time and celebrates when targets are met. Raids help amplify important posts, support partners, or drive visibility for project announcements. Admin Only Copy /raidx https://x.com/username/status/123456 Copy /raidx https://x.com/username/status/123456 likes=50 retweets=20 Copy /raidx https://x.com/username/status/123456 duration=1h mute=yes Parameters: Name Type Example post_url required https://x.com/username/status/123456 likes optional 50 retweets optional 20 replies optional 50 quotes optional 50 bookmarks optional 50 duration optional 1h mute optional yes hashtag /raid_status Displays real-time progress of active raids, showing current engagement numbers vs. targets for likes, retweets, replies, quotes, and bookmarks. You can check a specific raid by ID or see the latest active raid. Use this to monitor how your community is progressing toward raid goals and motivate participation. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example raid_id optional value hashtag /raid_stop Cancels an active raid immediately, stopping progress tracking and notifications. Use this when you need to end a raid early - for example, if the target post was deleted, targets were already met naturally, or the raid was started by mistake. The raid status will be marked as "CANCELLED" in the raid history. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example raid_id required value hashtag /raid_history Displays a list of previous raids with their completion status, targets, and results. Review past raid performance to see which raids were successful, how long they took, and engagement levels achieved. Useful for analyzing community participation patterns and planning future raids. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example limit optional value hashtag /set_raid_defaults Configures default settings for all future raids including target engagement numbers, duration, auto-mute behavior, and whether raids are enabled. These defaults are used when you start a raid without specifying custom parameters. Set these once to match your community's typical raid goals, then override them per-raid when needed with /raidx parameters. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example likes optional 30 retweets optional 15 replies optional 10 quotes optional 5 bookmarks optional 20 duration optional 1h mute optional no enabled optional yes hashtag /get_raid_defaults Displays the current default raid settings including target numbers for all engagement types, default duration, auto-mute behavior, and raid enable/disable status. Use this to review your configured defaults before starting a raid or to verify settings after using /set_raid_defaults. Admin Only hashtag /set_raid_message_behavior Controls how the bot updates raid progress messages in the chat. Choose between three modes: "edit" (updates the existing message cleanly), "repost" (deletes and resends to keep it at the bottom of the chat), or "pin" (updates and pins the message to keep it visible at the top). Different modes suit different community preferences and chat activity levels. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example mode required value hashtag /get_raid_message_behavior Shows the currently configured raid message update mode (edit, repost, or pin) and explains what that mode does. Use this to verify how raid progress messages will be displayed in your chat. Admin Only hashtag /set_raid_image Uploads a custom image that appears in all raid announcement messages for your community. After running this command, the bot will prompt you to send an image file. This branded image helps make raid announcements more visually appealing and recognizable to your community. The image is displayed prominently in every raid message. Admin Only hashtag /auto_raid_config Configures automatic raids that trigger for the "Best Tweet of the Day" selected during daily report generation. When enabled, the bot automatically starts a raid on the top-performing post a few minutes after the report is published. This helps consistently boost your best content without manual raid creation. Configure raid duration, delay before starting, target engagement numbers, and auto-mute behavior. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example enabled optional no duration optional 360 delay optional 5 likes optional 100 retweets optional 50 replies optional 50 quotes optional 50 bookmarks optional 50 mute optional yes hashtag /get_auto_raid_config Displays the current auto-raid configuration including whether it's enabled, the raid duration, start delay after report, target engagement numbers, and auto-mute setting. Use this to review your auto-raid setup or verify changes after using /auto_raid_config. paragraphs: Launches a coordinated community raid on an X (Twitter) post to boost engagement. Community members receive a raid announcement with the target post and engagement goals (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks). The bot tracks progress in real-time and celebrates when targets are met. Raids help amplify important posts, support partners, or drive visibility for project announcements.

Displays real-time progress of active raids, showing current engagement numbers vs. targets for likes, retweets, replies, quotes, and bookmarks. You can check a specific raid by ID or see the latest active raid. Use this to monitor how your community is progressing toward raid goals and motivate participation.

Cancels an active raid immediately, stopping progress tracking and notifications. Use this when you need to end a raid early - for example, if the target post was deleted, targets were already met naturally, or the raid was started by mistake. The raid status will be marked as "CANCELLED" in the raid history.

Displays a list of previous raids with their completion status, targets, and results. Review past raid performance to see which raids were successful, how long they took, and engagement levels achieved. Useful for analyzing community participation patterns and planning future raids.

Configures default settings for all future raids including target engagement numbers, duration, auto-mute behavior, and whether raids are enabled. These defaults are used when you start a raid without specifying custom parameters. Set these once to match your community's typical raid goals, then override them per-raid when needed with /raidx parameters.

Displays the current default raid settings including target numbers for all engagement types, default duration, auto-mute behavior, and raid enable/disable status. Use this to review your configured defaults before starting a raid or to verify settings after using /set_raid_defaults.

Controls how the bot updates raid progress messages in the chat. Choose between three modes: "edit" (updates the existing message cleanly), "repost" (deletes and resends to keep it at the bottom of the chat), or "pin" (updates and pins the message to keep it visible at the top). Different modes suit different community preferences and chat activity levels.
Shows the currently configured raid message update mode (edit, repost, or pin) and explains what that mode does. Use this to verify how raid progress messages will be displayed in your chat.

Uploads a custom image that appears in all raid announcement messages for your community. After running this command, the bot will prompt you to send an image file. This branded image helps make raid announcements more visually appealing and recognizable to your community. The image is displayed prominently in every raid message.

Configures automatic raids that trigger for the "Best Tweet of the Day" selected during daily report generation. When enabled, the bot automatically starts a raid on the top-performing post a few minutes after the report is published. This helps consistently boost your best content without manual raid creation. Configure raid duration, delay before starting, target engagement numbers, and auto-mute behavior.

Displays the current auto-raid configuration including whether it's enabled, the raid duration, start delay after report, target engagement numbers, and auto-mute setting. Use this to review your auto-raid setup or verify changes after using /auto_raid_config.

pages.title: Reports | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation for X Bot report generation commands. Covers viewing the most recent leaderboard report and triggering immediate report regeneration with latest X (Twitter) data. The /recreate command allows administrators to generate fresh reports outside the scheduled time and optionally clear report history.

codeExamples.description: Display the most recently generated leaderboard report with engagement statistics and rankings
Generate a fresh report immediately by fetching latest X data and creating new leaderboards
Generate a fresh report and delete all existing report history to start completely fresh

useCases: Share latest community performance data during investor presentations
Generate updated reports after adding new tracked accounts to filters
Verify tracking configuration is working correctly with immediate report
Reset report history when starting a new campaign or time period
Provide on-demand analytics to team members without waiting for scheduled reports

implementationSteps.title: View Latest Report
implementationSteps.description: Use /report command to instantly display the most recent leaderboard report including visual charts and formatted engagement statistics

implementationSteps.title: Generate Fresh Report
implementationSteps.description: Use /recreate to fetch current X data and generate updated leaderboards when you need reports outside the regular schedule

implementationSteps.title: Reset History (Optional)
implementationSteps.description: Use /recreate delete-history parameter to clear all existing reports and start with fresh baseline data for new tracking periods

Use this to quickly share the latest community performance data without waiting for the next scheduled report. Any community member can view reports - no admin privileges required.

pages.title: Schedule Management | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation for scheduling automated report generation in X Bot. Covers setting daily report schedules using simple time format or advanced cron expressions, viewing current schedule configuration, and deleting scheduled reports. Schedules are configured in UTC time and use AWS EventBridge for automation.

codeExamples.description: Schedule daily reports to generate automatically at 12:30 UTC every day
Schedule reports for specific days of the week at 14:30 UTC on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays
Use advanced cron expression for complex scheduling patterns like weekdays only at noon

useCases: Generate daily performance reports at team standup meeting times
Schedule weekly summary reports every Monday morning
Automate weekend reports for Monday review without manual intervention
Set multiple report times for different timezone audiences
Configure business-day-only reporting to exclude weekends

implementationSteps.title: Set Report Schedule
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_schedule with HH:MM format for simple daily scheduling or cron expressions for complex patterns, ensuring times are in UTC timezone

implementationSteps.title: Verify Configuration
implementationSteps.description: Use /get_schedule to confirm the scheduled time is configured correctly and reports will generate as expected

implementationSteps.title: Manage Schedule
implementationSteps.description: Use /delete_schedule to remove automated reporting if you need to change schedules or switch to manual report generation

paragraphs: Sets a schedule for when reports should be automatically generated. Supports both simple daily time scheduling (HH:MM format) and advanced cron expressions for complex schedules. Creates an EventBridge rule for scheduled report generation. Only group administrators can execute this command.
Retrieves and displays the current scheduled time for automatic report generation. Shows the configured UTC time or indicates if no schedule is set.

pages.title: Subscription & Credits | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation for X Bot subscription and payment management commands. Covers accessing Stripe customer portal for subscription management, initiating PRO subscription purchases, checking credit usage, and handling payment success/cancellation callbacks. Includes FREE tier credit limits and PRO unlimited usage.

codeExamples.description: Access Stripe customer portal for managing subscription, billing history, and payment methods
Initiate the purchase process for X Bot Professional subscription with Stripe checkout
Display current credit usage and availability, showing remaining credits for FREE tier or unlimited for PRO

useCases: Upgrade from FREE to PRO subscription for unlimited report generation
Monitor credit consumption on FREE tier before hitting limits
Access billing history and update payment methods through Stripe portal
Cancel or modify subscription settings without contacting support
Check credit balance before scheduling high-frequency automated reports

implementationSteps.title: Check Current Credits
implementationSteps.description: Use /get_credits to view remaining credits on FREE tier or confirm unlimited usage on PRO subscription

implementationSteps.title: Initiate Subscription Purchase
implementationSteps.description: Use /buy command in group chat to start PRO subscription purchase flow, which continues in private messages with Stripe integration

implementationSteps.title: Manage Subscription
implementationSteps.description: Use /subscription command to access Stripe customer portal for managing billing, payment methods, and subscription cancellation

pages.title: Topic Management | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation for Telegram forum topic management in X Bot. Covers configuring which forum topic thread should receive bot reports and updates in groups using Telegram's Topics (Forums) feature. Helps organize bot output in dedicated topics instead of cluttering main chat.

codeExamples.description: Configure the current forum topic to receive all bot reports and updates
Display which forum topic is currently configured to receive bot reports

useCases: Keep bot reports organized in a dedicated Analytics topic separate from general chat
Direct automated reports to a specific forum thread for admin review
Prevent bot notifications from cluttering main community discussions
Organize different bot functions across multiple forum topics
Maintain clean chat structure in forum-style Telegram groups

implementationSteps.title: Navigate to Target Topic
implementationSteps.description: Open the specific forum topic/thread where you want to receive bot reports in your Telegram group

implementationSteps.title: Configure Topic
implementationSteps.description: Run /set_topic command inside the desired topic to save it as the destination for all future reports and updates

implementationSteps.title: Verify Configuration
implementationSteps.description: Use /get_topic to confirm the correct forum topic is configured and all reports will be sent to the designated thread

pages.title: X Bot Installation | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Comprehensive installation and setup guide for X Bot with three distinct paths: Crypto Project Setup (track project engagement), Individual KOL Setup (showcase personal metrics), and KOL Team Setup (track team performance). Includes step-by-step instructions, verification procedures, and troubleshooting for common issues.

codeExamples.description: Track your official project X account during initial setup
Monitor posts mentioning your project's cashtag during configuration
Schedule automated daily analytics reports at 12:00 UTC

useCases: Track crypto project X engagement and run community competitions with prizes
Build public KOL portfolio on xbot.ninja to get discovered by projects
Monitor KOL team performance and demonstrate ROI to clients with verifiable data
Set up automated daily reports to keep community engaged with regular updates
Add project metadata to get featured on xbot.ninja for investor discovery

implementationSteps.title: Add Bot to Telegram Group
implementationSteps.description: Search for @BWS_X_Bot in Telegram, add it to your group, and make it an admin with required permissions

implementationSteps.title: Configure Tracking
implementationSteps.description: Use /add_accounts to track X accounts, /add_cashtags for token symbols, and /add_keywords for relevant topics based on your use case

implementationSteps.title: Schedule Reports
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_schedule to configure automated daily reports at times when your community is most active

implementationSteps.title: Verify Installation
implementationSteps.description: Run /recreate to generate your first report immediately, verify tracking is working correctly, and confirm all accounts are being monitored

implementationSteps.title: Add Metadata and Customize
implementationSteps.description: Optionally use /set_project_name, /set_project_description, /set_project_logo to get featured on xbot.ninja and customize report branding

You can customize tracking later. Track your official X account: Track your project's cashtag: Example: What this does: Tracks all posts from your project's X account Monitors posts mentioning your cashtag (e.g., $SOL ) Collects engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks, impressions) Includes tracked items in leaderboard rankings 3 Schedule Daily Reports Set up automatic daily analytics reports: What this does: Generates automated reports daily at 12:00 UTC Posts performance screenshots and leaderboards to your group Tracks top accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions Keeps community engaged with regular updates Tip: Choose a time when your community is most active for maximum engagement. 4 Verify Installation Force an initial report to verify everything is working: What this does: Creates an immediate performance report based on your configured tracking Shows leaderboards for accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions Confirms tracking is working correctly Gives you a preview of daily automated reports 5 Add Project Metadata (Optional) Add project information to be featured on the xbot.ninja arrow-up-right website: What this does: Displays your project on the xbot.ninja website for investor and community discovery Helps investors find projects with authentic community traction Showcases your project's performance metrics publicly 6 Customize Your Reports (Optional) Personalize reports with custom branding. paragraphs: This guide will walk you through installing and configuring X Bot for your Telegram group. The setup process differs based on your use case — choose the path that matches your needs.
Before installing X Bot, ensure you have:
Admin access to a Telegram group (or ability to create one)
An X (Twitter) account you want to track
Select the setup guide that matches your use case:
Goal: Track your project's X engagement and community activity (optionally use for competitions, prizes, or airdrops)
Add the bot to your project's main Telegram group
Make the bot an admin with the following settings:
Admin rights required: Yes
Special permissions needed: None
The following is a simple example for first-time installation. You can customize tracking later.
Track your official X account:
Track your project's cashtag:
Tracks all posts from your project's X account
Collects engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks, impressions)
Includes tracked items in leaderboard rankings
Set up automatic daily analytics reports:
Generates automated reports daily at 12:00 UTC
Tracks top accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions
Keeps community engaged with regular updates
Tip: Choose a time when your community is most active for maximum engagement.
Force an initial report to verify everything is working:
Creates an immediate performance report based on your configured tracking
Confirms tracking is working correctly
Gives you a preview of daily automated reports
Displays your project on the xbot.ninja website for investor and community discovery
Showcases your project's performance metrics publicly
Personalize reports with custom branding.
Set Custom Point Weights (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Bookmarks, Impressions):
Open Telegram and create a new group
Name it something like "My X Analytics" or "KOL Performance Tracker"
This can be a private group for personal tracking
Tip: Keep this group private or share it with potential clients to showcase your metrics.
Add the bot to your newly created group
Admin rights required: Yes
Special permissions needed: None
Monitor your personal X account performance:
Tracks all your posts and engagement metrics
Builds a verifiable portfolio of your performance
Enables discovery on xbot.ninja
Provides data for client reports
Track keywords relevant to your content:
Shows which topics you're covering
Tracks community engagement with your topics
Helps projects find you based on relevant keywords
Get daily performance insights:
Sends daily performance reports at 09:00 UTC
Helps identify top-performing content
Provides data for client reporting
Generate your first analytics report:
Creates an immediate snapshot of your performance
Verifies tracking is working correctly
Gives you shareable proof of your influence
Displays your KOL profile on xbot.ninja
Showcases engagement metrics and expertise publicly
To get publicly listed:
Maintain a minimum performance threshold by posting and engaging consistently
Build authentic engagement (avoid vanity metrics)
Monitor daily reports to track progress toward listing
Note: Featured KOLs are continuously monitored to maintain quality standards.
Goal: Track team performance accurately and show clients real, measurable results
Open Telegram and create a new group

Click the group name to open group info
Tap "Add Members"
Select all team members

Creates team-wide leaderboards
Enables performance comparisons

Identifies top performers per campaign

Tracks trends for each project
Provides regular updates for team and clients
Tip: Schedule for team standups or planning meetings.
Generate your first team analytics report:
Creates an immediate team performance snapshot
Verifies all accounts are tracked correctly
Provides shareable data for client presentations

Showcases team engagement metrics publicly

pages.summary: Overview of X Bot's purpose, features, and dual-platform approach serving project owners, KOLs, and investors. Explains the performance scoring algorithm, curation process for featured projects, and how X Bot solves crypto community engagement measurement problems using official X API for trusted analytics.

It enables tracking, analysis, and reporting of X activity directly within Telegram groups. X analytics you can trust X Bot uses the official X API to ensure accuracy and reliability. We tested several cheaper alternatives and even built our own "posts crawler," but none matched the accuracy of the official API. Many third-party analytics tools you find online show up to 50% discrepancies in their metrics. With X Bot, you get data directly from the source you can trust. hashtag The Problem X Bot Solves In the crypto space, it's challenging to: Get a trusted measure of community engagement and the real impact of hired KOLs' profitability Identify projects with genuine community traction vs. artificial engagement Find and hire KOL teams that deliver actual results Track and measure authentic X (Twitter) engagement Verify influencer performance with transparent metrics Discover legitimate investment opportunities backed by real community activity X Bot solves these problems by providing verifiable X analytics that distinguish authentic engagement from vanity metrics. hashtag Key Features hashtag Analytics & Tracking Track X (Twitter) accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions Monitor engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks, impressions) Generate automated scheduled reports with performance insights (up to 12-hour frequency) View leaderboards ranking top performers by engagement hashtag Performance Scoring X Bot uses a scoring algorithm that weighs different engagement types based on their value. The weights are fully configurable to match each project's specific needs and priorities. Example scoring formula (weights can be customized): Example Engagement Weights Explained: Likes (×1): Basic engagement, shows content appreciation Retweets (×2): Amplification - user shares content with their followers Replies (×1.5): Active conversation starter Quotes (×3): High-value engagement - adds commentary while sharing Bookmarks (×2): Content worth saving for later reference Impressions paragraphs: X Bot is a Telegram-based analytics platform that provides trusted X (Twitter) analytics for crypto communities. It enables tracking, analysis, and reporting of X activity directly within Telegram groups.
In the crypto space, it's challenging to:
Get a trusted measure of community engagement and the real impact of hired KOLs' profitability
Identify projects with genuine community traction vs. artificial engagement
Find and hire KOL teams that deliver actual results
Verify influencer performance with transparent metrics
Discover legitimate investment opportunities backed by real community activity
X Bot solves these problems by providing verifiable X analytics that distinguish authentic engagement from vanity metrics.
Track X (Twitter) accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions

Generate automated scheduled reports with performance insights (up to 12-hour frequency)

pages.title: Platform Fees | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Explains the fee structure for using BWS blockchain services, including how network fees are managed, test network allowances, and billing mechanisms. BWS handles all blockchain complexities including gas fees, token conversions, and charges customers using a credit-based system with 0.01 USD minimum billable units.
useCases: Calculate costs for creating NFTs on Polygon blockchain
Test blockchain solutions for free on Mumbai or Sepolia networks
Budget for blockchain integration projects using BWS API
Track monthly blockchain credit usage and billing
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right Fees Calculator API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Platform Fees We take care of everything. Calling blockchain smart contracts requires funds: “It is the fuel that allows it to operate”. circle-info Fees compensate miners for their computational efforts in processing and securing transactions on the network. They are fundamental to the blockchain's sustainability, security, and efficiency, ensuring the network remains robust and decentralized. Blockchain Web Services (BWS) aims to transform the blockchain market by making innovative solutions accessible to a broad audience, including those without technical or Web3 experience. hashtag Fees Calculator Interacting with blockchain requires paying fees, so it's important to understand how Blockchain Web Services manages those fees to offer our customers the best user experience. circle-check In any scenario, we handle all the blockchain complexities. You interact with the blockchain(s) we support using our simplified API, and you don't need to worry about anything other than naming the blockchain you want to use. If you haven't entered your credit card, you can test our blockchain solutions for free using the Mumbai or Sepolia test networks. You can run 100 blockchain transactions for free. We use our token pools to pay network fees, and you are not charged. Using test networks does not consume $BWS tokens. Once you move into subscription mode by entering your credit card details, You can run 1000 testnet transactions for free. When running on main networks, we do real-time trading, convert US dollars to the required network token, and pay the fees for you. You will then be charged for those fees on your next bill, using 0.01 USD as the minimum billable unit. Every solution requires $BWS tokens to execute and fund the developer's community and platform maintenance. Please read the platform White Paper arrow-up-right to know more about the $BWS token and its tokenomics. hashtag Pricing Example Suppose you subscribe to Blockchain Web Services and want to create 100 new NFTs on the Polygon blockchain using BWS.NFT.zK arrow-up-right to create the NFTs, and BWS.IPFS.Upload arrow-up-right to save your images to IPFS. Creating those 100 NFTs will cost you: Your next bill will show an extra 2 USD to your monthly plan. hashtag How do I pay those Fees? To check your current monthly cost, go to the Billing menu and check your credit usage. circle-info BLOCKCHAIN CREDIT FEE We use the Blockchain Credit Fee as the minimum billable unit, 0.01 USD. If, for example, you spend 2 USD creating NFTs, we will add 200 blockchain credit fee units to your next bill. Credits Usage balance. Previous Quick Start chevron-left Next API How Tos chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago Fees Calculator Pricing Example How do I pay those Fees? sun-bright desktop moon Copy 100 IPFS Images, included = 0 USD 100 NFTs x 0.02 Network Fee = 2 USD sun-bright desktop moon

paragraphs: We take care of everything.
Calling blockchain smart contracts requires funds: “It is the fuel that allows it to operate”.

Fees compensate miners for their computational efforts in processing and securing transactions on the network. They are fundamental to the blockchain's sustainability, security, and efficiency, ensuring the network remains robust and decentralized.
Blockchain Web Services (BWS) aims to transform the blockchain market by making innovative solutions accessible to a broad audience, including those without technical or Web3 experience.
Interacting with blockchain requires paying fees, so it's important to understand how Blockchain Web Services manages those fees to offer our customers the best user experience.

In any scenario, we handle all the blockchain complexities. You interact with the blockchain(s) we support using our simplified API, and you don't need to worry about anything other than naming the blockchain you want to use.
If you haven't entered your credit card, you can test our blockchain solutions for free using the Mumbai or Sepolia test networks.
You can run 100 blockchain transactions for free.
We use our token pools to pay network fees, and you are not charged.
Using test networks does not consume $BWS tokens.
Once you move into subscription mode by entering your credit card details,
You can run 1000 testnet transactions for free.

When running on main networks, we do real-time trading, convert US dollars to the required network token, and pay the fees for you. You will then be charged for those fees on your next bill, using 0.01 USD as the minimum billable unit.
Creating those 100 NFTs will cost you:
Your next bill will show an extra 2 USD to your monthly plan.
To check your current monthly cost, go to the Billing menu and check your credit usage.

pages.title: Quick Start | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Quick start guide demonstrating how to write 'Hello World!' to the Matchain blockchain in one API call using BWS.Blockchain.Hash. Covers authentication, making the first API request, and checking job status asynchronously to receive blockchain receipts and Certificate of Trust.

codeExamples.description: Write 'Hello World!' to Matchain blockchain using BWS.Blockchain.Hash insert operation
Check blockchain job status and retrieve transaction receipt using jobId
JavaScript example using jQuery AJAX to save data to blockchain

useCases: Write first message to blockchain without managing wallets
Test blockchain integration with simple API calls
Store immutable data records on Matchain network
Generate Certificate of Trust for blockchain transactions

implementationSteps.title: Get your API key
implementationSteps.description: Obtain authentication credentials by creating an account and generating your API key for authenticated requests.

implementationSteps.title: Write data to blockchain
implementationSteps.description: Call the BWS.Blockchain.Hash API with solution, network, operation (insert), and parameters containing key-value data to save.

implementationSteps.title: Check for job completion
implementationSteps.description: Use the returned jobId to fetch blockchain job status and results, including transaction receipt and Certificate of Trust.

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Quick Start Writing "Hello World!" to the blockchain in just one call! hashtag Get your API key Your API requests should be authenticated using your API key (any request that doesn't include an API key will return an error). circle-info Learn how to Get your API Key . hashtag Your First BWS API Request We're going to use the BWS.Blockchain.Hash arrow-up-right solution to save a message to the blockchain. hashtag Write "Hello World!" to Matchain blockchain. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/smart-contracts/call Let's write "Hello World!" to the Matchain blockchain network without managing network fees or Web3 wallets by calling a regular Web2 API. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.Blockchain.Hash version * number 1 network * string matchain operation * string insert parameters * JSON { key: "mykey", value: "Hello World!" } Please copy & Paste one of the following code snippets and run it (remember to use your API Key). curl Javascript Phyton If executed correctly, check for jobId in the response body part. We will use it to verify that the blockchain operation has been completed correctly and to receive the blockchain transaction receipt and Certificate of Trust . API Call response hashtag Check for Status To check for job completion and results, use the jobId you got from the previous call. circle-info Please note that interacting with blockchain(s) is asynchronous, so it may take some time for the operation to finish and for the blockchain receipt to be received. hashtag Fetch for the blockchain job status and results. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/smart-contracts/fetch Using the previous call response jobId we fetch the blockchain job started previously. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description jobId * String (use previously returned jobId, as for example 59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e) Copy & Paste one of the following code snippets and run it (remember to use your own API Key). curl Javascript Phyton You should receive a response indicating the completed job and the related blockchain receipt and certificate. Fetch API Call Response Congratulations! In just one call, you used BWS to save data into the Web3 blockchain ecosystem. Previous Welcome! chevron-left Next Platform Fees chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago Get your API key Your First BWS API Request Write "Hello World!" to Matchain blockchain. Check for Status Fetch for the blockchain job status and results. sun-bright desktop moon Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/smart-contracts/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.Blockchain.Hash", "version": 1, "network": "matchain", "operation": "insert", "parameters": { "key": "timestamp: 1736418591440", "value": "Hola" } }' Copy var settings = { "url": "https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call", "method": "POST", "timeout": 0, "headers": { "X-Api-Key": "API-KEY", "Content-Type": "application/json" }, "data": JSON.stringify({ "solution": "BWS.Blockchain.Hash", "version": 1, "network": "matchain", "operation": "insert", "parameters": { "key": "timestamp: 1736418591440", "value": "Hola" } }), }; $.ajax(settings).done(function (response) { console.log(response); }); Copy import http.client import json conn = http.client.HTTPSConnection("api.bws.ninja") payload = json.dumps({ "solution": "BWS.Blockchain.Hash", "version": 1, "network": "matchain", "operation": "insert", "parameters": { "key": "timestamp: 1736418591440", "value": "Hola" } }) headers = { 'X-Api-Key': 'API-KEY', 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } conn.request("POST", "/v1/call", payload, headers) res = conn.getresponse() data = res.read() print(data.decode("utf-8")) Copy { "statusCode": 200, "statusMessage": "", "info": { "jobId": "59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e" } } Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/fetch' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "jobId": "59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e" }' Copy var settings = { "url": "https://api.bws.ninja/v1/fetch", "method": "POST", "timeout": 0, "headers": { "X-Api-Key": "API-KEY", "Content-Type": "application/json" }, "data": JSON.stringify({ "jobId": "59d0f46b-0b58-4512-8aca-51d06f69f25e" }), }; paragraphs: Writing "Hello World!" to the blockchain in just one call!
Your API requests should be authenticated using your API key (any request that doesn't include an API key will return an error).
Let's write "Hello World!" to the Matchain blockchain network without managing network fees or Web3 wallets by calling a regular Web2 API.
Please copy & Paste one of the following code snippets and run it (remember to use your API Key).
Please note that interacting with blockchain(s) is asynchronous, so it may take some time for the operation to finish and for the blockchain receipt to be received.

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right Solution Overview Operations BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down PLATFORM APIs chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash Solution Overview Use the blockchain to efficiently store data in blockchain(s). BWS.Blockchain.Hash simplifies saving data to the blockchain, enabling you to use it as a hash database while abstracting the complexities of blockchain infrastructure. hashtag What is a Hash Database? A hash database is a type of database that uses hash keys to efficiently retrieve and store data. Each record is assigned a unique key, often derived from the data or predefined attributes, which acts as an identifier for quick lookups. Hash databases are known for their speed and scalability, making them ideal for applications requiring rapid data access. hashtag An example: saving client data to the blockchain. Businesses can use BWS.Blockchain.Hash to manage client data by storing essential information (e.g., customer profiles or transaction records) in a blockchain-based hash database. The system uses unique keys, such as client IDs or transaction numbers, to retrieve data effortlessly. Here's how this could work: Client Information Use a unique client ID as the hash key to link to stored client details or references. Data Retrieval Quickly access data associated with a hash key for reporting, auditing, or verification purposes. Enhanced Trust Leverage blockchain’s immutable nature to ensure the integrity and transparency of stored records. hashtag Why Choose BWS.Blockchain.Hash? This solution is perfect for individuals and businesses that want to take advantage of blockchain as a trusted and efficient hash-based storage system. With BWS.Blockchain.Hash , you can store and retrieve data securely, gain the benefits of blockchain transparency, and provide clients with a Certificate of Trust for each entry—ensuring their data is handled responsibly and verifiably. hashtag Available Networks circle-exclamation We're currently updating this solution smart contracts for Polygon (main) and Mumbai (test). Please use Matchain. implementationSteps.title: Prepare file content

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right Solution Overview Operations BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down PLATFORM APIs chevron-right BWS.IPFS.Upload Operations Easily upload Images, PDF and JSON files to IPFS. hashtag Upload to IPFS Use this operation to upload content to IPFS and get the IPFS Content Identifier (CID). POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.IPFS.Upload operation * string new parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the IPFS CID and useful URIs. Copy { "statusCode" : 200 , "info" : { "cid" : "QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" , "uris" : { "ipfs" : "ipfs://QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" , "url" : "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" } } } hashtag Upload to IPFS Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption description string Short description of your content (will be returned when listing your IPFS files ). content JSON / base64 Content to save on the IPFS network. We currently support: JSON (just pass the JSON or the stringified string) File sent as a base64 encoded string circle-exclamation Please note that when uploading your file: You must optimize your content to be less than 1 MB in size (there is a hard limit of 5MB when uploading files to IPFS using our API). We currently support JSON, images, and PDF files. If your use case requires higher file sizes or other file types, please contact us. hashtag Upload to IPFS Examples Upload an Image using nodejs Upload JSON using cURL hashtag Upload to IPFS call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the IPFS Content Identifier (CID) along with the corresponding IPFS URI and URL. These details facilitate easy access and reference to the uploaded content on the IPFS network. hashtag List IPFS Files Get the list of your IPFS files POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.IPFS.Upload operation * string list parameters JSON check Method Parameters 200 The list of files you uploaded to IPFS hashtag List IPFS Files Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption from long Unix time arrow-up-right in milliseconds representing a timestamp to select IPFS files from. to long Unix time arrow-up-right in milliseconds representing a timestamp to select IPFS files to. hashtag LIST IPFS Files Request Example cURL hashtag List IPFS Files call Response Once executed correctly, you will get a list of the IPFS uploaded files using BWS API. That list will contain for each file: the timestamp of creation ( Unix time arrow-up-right in milliseconds), the IPFS Content Identifier (CID), the file type, and the URIs to easily access the file IPFS URI, web URL - using the BWS IPFS Gateway to fetch the file. Previous Solution Overview chevron-left Next BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago Upload to IPFS Request Body Upload to IPFS call Response List IPFS Files Request Body List IPFS Files call Response sun-bright desktop moon Copy const fs = require('fs'); const axios = require('axios'); /* get the file you want to upload to IPFS */ const fileData = fs.readFileSync('./files/image.png'); /* encode file content to base64 */ const encodedData = fileData.toString('base64'); /* build request to use BWS IPFS solution */ const request = { "solution": "BWS.IPFS.Upload", "operation": "new", "parameters": { description: "My first IPFS file using BWS API!", parameters: { content: encodedData } } }; /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method: 'post', maxBodyLength: Infinity, url: 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call', headers: { 'X-Api-Key': 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v', /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, data : JSON.stringify(request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console.log(JSON.stringify(response.data)); }) .catch((error) => { console.log(error); }); Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: XqaLg...729v' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.IPFS.Upload", "operation": "new", "parameters": { "description": "Just a simple JSON for testing BWS API", "content": { "text": "Hello World!" } } }' Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "cid": "QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN", "uris": { "ipfs": "ipfs://QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN", "url": "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" } } } Copy { "statusCode": 200, "statusMessage": "", "info": [ { "": "" } ] } Copy curl --location 'https://api.staging.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: XqaLg...729v' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.IPFS.Upload", "operation": "list" "parameters": { "from": 1700404140000 "to": 1700411940000 } }' Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": [ { "timestamp": "1700416540067", "cid": "QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN", "description": "Just a simple JSON for testing BWS API", "fileType": "json", "uris": { "ipfs": "ipfs://QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN", "url": "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" } }, { "timestamp": "1700121600009", "cid": "QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu", "description": "image for token 1325", "fileType": "image", "uris": { "ipfs": "ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu", "url": "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu" } } ] } sun-bright desktop moon

pages.summary: Documentation for BWS IPFS Upload service that enables decentralized file storage using InterPlanetary File System (IPFS). Supports uploading images, PDF, and JSON files with content addressing and global accessibility through the ipfs.bws.ninja gateway.
useCases: Store NFT metadata and asset images for blockchain applications
Host decentralized content delivery for logos and graphics
Share structured JSON data with transparency and verifiability

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right Solution Overview Operations BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down PLATFORM APIs chevron-right BWS.IPFS.Upload Solution Overview Easily upload Images, PDF and JSON files to IPFS. BWS IPFS API Service leverages the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) , a distributed, peer-to-peer storage protocol designed to create a permanent and decentralized method of storing and sharing data. IPFS breaks files into smaller chunks, distributes them across the network, and assigns each file a unique content identifier (CID) for efficient retrieval. hashtag BWS IPFS Gateway Use ipfs.bws.ninja as the gateway to access the files you have uploaded to IPFS using BWS. circle-info An IPFS gateway acts as a bridge between the decentralized IPFS network and traditional web browsers, allowing users to access content stored on IPFS using standard HTTP protocols. This makes IPFS content easily accessible without requiring specialized software or configuration. Check out our lovely unicorn on IPFS using the BWS Gateway: https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmU7avmnTb4iVbNGCHMJmdEbsx9nRtUx7dWGpKeE7zjo8T arrow-up-right hashtag Key Features of IPFS Decentralized Storage Files are stored across multiple nodes, enhancing reliability and fault tolerance. Content Addressing Data is retrieved using its CID, ensuring that files are immutable and tamper-proof. Global Accessibility IPFS data can be accessed by any node in the network, fostering collaboration and resilience. hashtag Supported File Types Our service supports uploading images. PDF and JSON files , making it ideal for use cases where these formats are predominant. Examples include: NFT Metadata and Assets Storing JSON metadata files and associated images for blockchain applications. Decentralized Content Delivery Hosting static resources like logos or graphics for web or app projects. Data Integrity and Provenance Sharing structured data (e.g., JSON) in a transparent, verifiable way. pages.title: NFT Attributes (traits) | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Explains NFT attributes (traits) structure and implementation for making NFTs unique. Details the metadata format including trait_type and value pairs, with examples from digital art collections showing how traits like fur color and accessories define NFT characteristics.

codeExamples.description: Example NFT metadata JSON structure showing how to define traits for a digital animated cat NFT with multiple attributes

useCases: Define unique characteristics for digital art NFT collections
Create rarity-based traits that influence NFT value
Structure metadata for animated character collections
Implement trait-based filtering and discovery
Establish categorical attributes for collectible NFTs
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Solution Overview chevron-right Operations chevron-right NFT Attributes (traits) Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down PLATFORM APIs chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK NFT Attributes (traits) Making your NFT unique. Non-fungible tokens (NFTs) represent unique digital assets on blockchain platforms. Those assets can define associated traits , which are specific attributes or characteristics that detail each NFT. In a practical scenario, an NFT's metadata might look something like this for a digital art collection of animated cats: Copy { "name" : "Animated Cat #001" , "description" : "A unique animated cat with distinct features." , "image" : "ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu" , "attributes" : [ { "trait_type" : "Fur Color" , "value" : "Black" } , { "trait_type" : "Eye Type" , "value" : "Round" } , { "trait_type" : "Accessories" , "value" : "Hat" } ] } Within the metadata, the attributes array contains the various traits of the NFT. Each trait is defined by a trait_type , which specifies the category of the trait (e.g., "Fur Color" or "Rarity"), and a value , which indicates the specific characteristic of that trait (e.g., "Black" or "Ultra-Rare"). Traits encapsulated within this metadata not only bring out the uniqueness of each token but can also influence its perceived value, especially if some traits are considered rarer than others. BWS.NFT.zK allows you to create an NFT by passing those traits . circle-exclamation Please note that it's the usage of those traits when visualizing your NFT that you must take care of and validate. For example, if you use the provided Certificate of Trust for your users' experience use case, you must check they are correctly displayed. Previous Operations chevron-left Next Blockchain Badges chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago sun-bright desktop moon sun-bright desktop moon

pages.title: Operations | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Complete API reference for NFT.zK operations including Create NFT, List NFTs, Transfer NFT, and Send NFT by Email. Provides detailed request/response formats, method parameters, and cURL examples for each asynchronous blockchain operation with job tracking.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new NFT on the Mumbai test network with name, description, image URL, and rarity attributes
Response from NFT creation API call returning a job ID for tracking the asynchronous blockchain transaction

useCases: Mint new NFTs on Polygon or Ethereum blockchains
List and retrieve all NFTs associated with an account
Transfer NFT ownership to wallet addresses
Send NFTs to recipients via email without requiring wallet
Track NFT creation and transfer with transaction hashes

implementationSteps.title: Create NFT
implementationSteps.description: Call the API with solution 'BWS.NFT.zK', operation 'new', and parameters including name, description, image URL/IPFS URI, and optional attributes. Returns a jobId for tracking.

implementationSteps.title: Check Transaction Status
implementationSteps.description: Use the fetch API with the provided jobId to check blockchain transaction status and retrieve NFT details including transaction hash, IPFS hashes, and transfer codes.

implementationSteps.title: Transfer Ownership
implementationSteps.description: Use 'transfer' operation with nftId and wallet address, or 'send' operation with nftId and email address to transfer NFT ownership to new owners.

Check the blockchain transaction status and receipt using the fetch API and the provided jobId. hashtag Creates a new NFT. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to create (Mint) a new NFT on a selected blockchain Network. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK version * number 1 network * string check available networks arrow-up-right operation * string new parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id Copy { "statusCode" : 200 , "statusMessage" : "" , "info" : { "jobId" : "59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e" } } hashtag Create NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Name of your NFT. description string The NFT description. image URL (or IPFS URI) This is the URL or IPFS URI of your NFT image. attributes optional JSON These are the attributes of your NFT. hashtag Create NFT Request Example cURL hashtag Create NFT Fetch Response chevron-right Create NFT Fetch Response Example hashtag The fetch operation , once the NFT creation job is completed, will return the following: Parameter Type Description guid string NFT unique id network string Network the NFT has been created in. nftTxHash string Blockchain transaction hash nftId string NFT id nftIPFSHash string NFT metadata file IPFS hash nftImageIPFSHash string NFT image file IPFS hash nftJson json NFT metadata json url json (check NFT Urls ) status string The NFT status: "created" or "transferred" transferCodes json (check Transfer Codes ) hashtag NFT Urls hashtag Easy-to-use URLs for you to get access to NFT's most relevant data. Parameter Type Description nft URL NFT metada URL image URL NFT image URL transaction URL NFT blockchain transaction explorer URL hashtag Transfer Codes hashtag Transfer codes that are required to transfer ownership when sending an NFT through email (check NFT Ownership to learn more). Parameter Type Description owner string Owner code is required to execute a transfer when sending an NFT over email. receiver string The receiver code the new owner should indicate to execute the NFT transfer. hashtag List NFTs hashtag Lists your NFTs. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Get the list of your NFTs. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK operation * string list parameters JSON check Methods Parameters 200 Returns the list of your NFTs. hashtag List NFT Method Parameters circle-info If no range is defined, the last 20 NFTs are returned. Parameter Type Desciption from long Number of milliseconds representing a timestamp to select NFTs from. to long Number of milliseconds representing a timestamp to select NFTs to. hashtag List IPFS Files call Response hashtag LIST NFT Request Example cURL hashtag List NFT Response The list operation returns the list of your NFTs, including NFT-relevant data and the network you created the NFT in. chevron-right List NFT Fetch Response Example hashtag hashtag Transfer NFT circle-info This operation is asynchronous. (once executed, check the results using fetch API and the provided jobId) hashtag Transfer an NFT ownership. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Transfer an NFT ownership by using the new owner's wallet or email address. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK version * number 1 network * string check available networks arrow-up-right operation * string transfer parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id hashtag Transfer NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption nftId string The unique identifier of the NFT to be transferred. address string Wallet address to send the NFT to (check NFT Owerneship ) hashtag Transfer NFT Request Example cURL hashtag Transfer NFT Fetch Response The fetch operation for the NFT transfer API call will mainly state if the transfer has been successful ( completed ) and the related costs. chevron-right Transfer NFT Fetch Response Example hashtag hashtag Send NFT by Email hashtag Send an NFT by email. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Send an NFT by using the new owner's email address. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK network * string check available networks arrow-up-right operation * string send parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id hashtag Send NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption nftId string The unique identifier of the NFT to be transferred. email string Email address to send the NFT to (check NFT Owerneship ) hashtag Send NFT Request Example cURL hashtag Send NFT by Email Response As a confirmation message response, if the NFT send is confirmed you will get your NFT-related details, including the codes that the new owner will have to use to execute a blockchain NFT transfer. chevron-right Transfer NFT Fetch Response Example hashtag Previous Solution Overview chevron-left Next NFT Attributes (traits) chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago Create NFT Creates a new NFT. Transfer NFT Method Parameters Transfer NFT Fetch Response Send NFT by Email Send an NFT by email. pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Overview of NFT.zK solution covering NFT ownership models, transfer methods, and metadata storage. Explains custody through BWS account, transfer options via wallet or email, and how NFT images and metadata are stored on IPFS across supported networks.

codeExamples.description: Example NFT creation request using a URL for the NFT image, which will be automatically uploaded to IPFS
Example NFT metadata structure using IPFS URI for the image, showing how NFT data is stored on IPFS

useCases: Transfer NFT ownership to blockchain wallet addresses
Send NFTs to users via email without requiring Web3 wallets
Store NFT images and metadata on IPFS for decentralized access
Manage NFT custody through BWS account before transfer
Create NFTs on Matchain, Polygon, or Ethereum networks

implementationSteps.title: Create NFT with BWS Custody
implementationSteps.description: Create a new NFT using BWS.NFT.zK which will be initially linked to your BWS account with custody managed by BWS.

implementationSteps.title: Provide Image URL or IPFS URI
implementationSteps.description: Pass the NFT image as either a web-addressable URL or IPFS URI. BWS will automatically upload to IPFS if needed and create metadata.

implementationSteps.title: Transfer Ownership
implementationSteps.description: Transfer NFT ownership either to a blockchain wallet address using the Transfer operation, or to an email address allowing the recipient to claim it later.

paragraphs: When you create a new NFT using BWS.NFT.zK (Zero Knowledge) solution, the NFT will initially be linked to your BWS account. In order to transfer an NFT ownership, you have two options:
Transfer ownership to a blockchain wallet address.
Transfer ownership to an email address.
Before you transfer your NFT, we will do the custody, and ownership is mapped with your BWS Account.
If you want to transfer an NFT ownership, but the new owner does not have a Web3 Wallet (or you don't know it), you can simply transfer the ownership by using his email address.
We will send the new owner an email notification indicating he's the owner of the NFT. If he wants to transfer the ownership to his own Wallet, he will be able to.
Please note that once you transfer the ownership using an email address, ONLY the email address owner will be able to transfer the NFT to a new Wallet address.
To create an NFT using BWS.NFT.zK, you pass the NFT image as:
a web addressable URL, or,
as an IPFS URI (for example, ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowki).
API Call JSON Example using a URL for the NFT image.
In all the scenarios, your NFT metadata will point to an image stored in IPFS, as we will upload your image to IPFS if required.
When a new NFT is created, we create and upload the NFT metadata to IPFS,
